# COACH NAVIGATION + MENUS AUDIT — EVA (web)

## 1. Architecture of the nav (3 files)

- **Registry (single source of truth):** `apps/web/src/components/coach/coach-nav.ts` — `NAV_MODULES` array (coach-nav.ts:45-65). Pure functions `getVisibleNavItems(ctx)` (coach-nav.ts:92) and `splitNavItems(items)` (coach-nav.ts:125). Already supports an `entitlement?: ModuleKey` hook (coach-nav.ts:40) and partitions visible items into `core` vs `modules`.
- **Renderer:** `apps/web/src/components/coach/CoachSidebar.tsx` — one component renders BOTH the desktop left sidebar AND the mobile chrome (top header + bottom bar). It calls `getVisibleNavItems` (CoachSidebar.tsx:100) + `splitNavItems` (CoachSidebar.tsx:103).
- **Shell:** `apps/web/src/app/coach/layout.tsx` resolves the active workspace + `enabledModules` server-side and passes them to `CoachSidebar` (layout.tsx:147-163). `CoachMainWrapper.tsx` is just the `<main>` content wrapper (special-cases builder + nutrition-hub width).

Visibility model already in code: **visible = in `contexts` for active workspace AND (if `entitlement` set) `enabledModules[entitlement] === true`** (coach-nav.ts:105-112). The real gate is server-side `assertModule`; the nav is a visual mirror, default-OFF. This is exactly the "ENTITLED AND ENABLED" model the founder wants — the per-coach toggle layer (`coach_feature_prefs`) would slot in as a third AND-narrowing condition here.

## 2. Complete menu inventory

### Top-level nav items (registry `NAV_MODULES`, coach-nav.ts:45-65)

| key | label (desktop / mobile short) | href | contexts | entitlement gate |

| dashboard | Dashboard / Inicio | `/coach/dashboard` | all | — |

### Count of distinct top-level menus a standalone coach sees on a normal plan
Dashboard, Alumnos, Programas, Ejercicios, Nutrición, Mi Marca, Suscripción, Soporte = **8 base** + up to 2 module items (Cardio, Movimiento) = **up to 10**. On mobile these all live in ONE horizontally-scrolling bottom bar.

### "Mi Marca" / Opciones hub sub-pages (`/coach/settings/*`)
The settings page is **context-aware** (settings/page.tsx):
- **Standalone** (settings/page.tsx:182): title "Personaliza la app de tus alumnos", contains → link **Módulos** (`/coach/settings/modules`), link **Áreas del builder** (`/coach/settings/areas`), `LogoUploadForm`, `BrandSettingsForm`, `DangerZone`.
- **team_managed** (settings/page.tsx:26-81): title "Opciones", 3 cards → Módulos, Áreas, Mi Equipo (`/coach/team`) + DangerZone. **This is already a "3-card hub" prototype** — the visual pattern the founder wants for the new Opciones hub.
- **No-branding tier** (settings/page.tsx:86): upsell-only screen + DangerZone.

Sub-pages:
- `/coach/settings/modules` — **READ-ONLY catalog** (`ModulesForm`, modules/page.tsx + ModulesForm.tsx). No toggles anymore — switches were removed (compra-only). Per module: Activo/Disponible badge + pitch + surfaces + CTA (mailto or self-service). Back-link → "Opciones" or "Mi Equipo". **This is the natural home / sibling for the new "Funciones" enable/disable zone.**
- `/coach/settings/areas` — `AreasManager`, custom builder areas. Back-link → Opciones/Mi Equipo.

### Nutrición is itself a hub with internal tabs (NutritionHub.tsx)
Tabs (TabsTrigger, NutritionHub.tsx:164-192): Plantillas, Activos (planes), **Alimentos** (`foods` tab → embeds `FoodLibrary`), **Recetas** (`recipes` tab → embeds `RecipeLibrary`). So Alimentos and Recetas each exist in TWO places: as the embedded tab here AND as standalone routes `/coach/foods` + `/coach/recipes`.

## 3. Responsive / mobile behavior

One `CoachSidebar` drives all breakpoints (CoachSidebar.tsx):
- **Desktop (`md:`):** fixed left sidebar, collapsible (20rem ↔ 5rem) with state persisted to `localStorage 'sidebar-collapsed'` (CoachSidebar.tsx:74-77, 85-89). Core items on top; if any purchased modules → a **"MÓDULOS" divider** (CoachSidebar.tsx:283-303, desktop-only). Bottom area: news bell, theme toggle, workspace switcher, sign-out, privacy link.
- **Mobile (`<md`):** TWO fixed chromes —
  1. **Top header** (CoachSidebar.tsx:149-185): brand icon+name, WorkspaceSwitcher, news bell, theme toggle, sign-out, and a context HelpCircle on `/coach/settings`.
  2. **Bottom bar** (CoachSidebar.tsx:188-306): the SAME nav links rendered **flat `[...core, ...modules]` in one horizontal scroll** — no hamburger, no overflow menu, no grouping. Up to 10 icon+short-label tiles (min-w 3.5rem each) scroll horizontally with animated chevron scroll-hint indicators (CoachSidebar.tsx:241-262). **This is the core clutter problem:** on a phone the coach gets a single crammed horizontally-scrolling strip of 8-10 tiles with no prioritization.
- **Builder routes** hide both mobile chromes (`isBuilder`, CoachSidebar.tsx:97, 190-194) to avoid double fixed bars.

## 4. Clutter / overlap / duplication findings

1. **Mobile bottom bar = flat horizontal scroll of up to 10 items, no overflow/"More" menu.** The single biggest clutter source. Nothing collapses; everything competes.
2. **`Ejercicios` is a full top-level menu reachable ONLY from the nav** — grep confirms zero in-app cross-links to `/coach/exercises` anywhere (only the sidebar). Demoting it costs nothing elsewhere. It is a peer of `Programas` but conceptually subordinate (you pick exercises while building programs).
3. **Alimentos duplicated:** standalone `/coach/foods` route AND the "Alimentos" tab inside Nutrición hub render the same `FoodLibrary`/`getFoodLibrary`. The foods page even links back to the hub. `/coach/foods` is not in the nav (orphan route).
4. **Recetas duplicated + half-dead:** `/coach/recipes` is a bare `redirect → /coach/foods`, yet Nutrición hub has a real "Recetas" tab; `/coach/recipes/[recipeId]` detail still lives. Inconsistent.
5. **`/coach/templates` is a dead redirect** to workout-programs (legacy).
6. **Two settings entry labels for one route:** `brand` ("Mi Marca") and `settings_team` ("Opciones") both → `/coach/settings`, differing only by context. The page already renders a 3-card hub in team mode — the founder's "Opciones hub with 3 big buttons" partially exists here.
7. **`/coach/settings/modules` is read-only** (toggles removed). There is currently NO coach-facing enable/disable surface — the planned "Funciones" zone has no home yet; modules page is the logical sibling.
8. **Nutrición page** mixes tier-gate upsell, hub, org templates, and 4 internal tabs — heavy single surface.

## 5. Ejercicios → Programas merge assessment (concrete, low-risk)

- **Both pages are independent server pages** with their own `_data` queries; neither imports the other. `/coach/exercises` (exercises/page.tsx) renders `ExerciseCatalogClient`; `/coach/workout-programs` (workout-programs/page.tsx) renders `WorkoutProgramsClientShell → WorkoutProgramsClient`.
- **Zero inbound links to `/coach/exercises`** except the sidebar item (grep returned no matches outside coach-nav.ts). So removing the nav entry breaks no in-app navigation — only the menu tile disappears.
- **Natural button location:** `LibraryHeader.tsx` (the Programas page header) already has a right-aligned action ("Nueva plantilla" `<Button>`, LibraryHeader.tsx:57-64). A second, **different-colored** "Lista de ejercicios" button (`variant="outline"` / secondary) sits naturally beside it. The page keeps `/coach/exercises` as the route; only the entry point moves from nav → button.
- **Safety:** keep `/coach/exercises` route alive (don't delete — bookmarks/deep links). Just drop the `exercises` entry from `NAV_MODULES` (coach-nav.ts:50) and add the button. The `splitNavItems`/`getVisibleNavItems` purity + existing tests mean removing one registry entry is a contained change.
- **Same pattern applies to:** `/coach/foods` (fold into Nutrición as it already is — drop the orphan route or make Nutrición the only entry), and the dead redirects `/coach/templates` + `/coach/recipes` (already done implicitly).

## 6. Where the founder's 3 goals plug in (no code, just anchors)

- **Goal 1 (consolidate):** drop `exercises` from `NAV_MODULES`; mobile bottom bar needs an overflow/"More" grouping instead of flat scroll. The `core`/`modules` split (splitNavItems) already exists to drive grouping.
- **Goal 2 (per-coach + per-client enable/disable, generic `coach_feature_prefs`/`client_feature_prefs`):** slots into `getVisibleNavItems` (coach-nav.ts:105-112) as a third AND condition after `contexts` and `entitlement`. Server gate mirrors today's `assertModule` pattern. The toggle UI's natural home is `/coach/settings/modules` (currently read-only) renamed/extended as "Funciones".
- **Goal 3 (Opciones hub, 3 buttons):** the `team_managed` branch of settings/page.tsx (lines 26-81) is already a 3-card hub — generalize it to all contexts: **Mi Marca** (existing branding form, split out), **Suscripción** (`/coach/subscription`), **Opciones Coach** (NEW, hosts the Funciones zone). Replace the separate `brand`/`billing`/`settings_team` nav entries with one **Opciones** entry → this hub.

# EVA Modules + Entitlements + Settings/Menu Audit

## 1. Coach top-level menu inventory (single source of truth)

The entire coach menu is **registry-driven** from `apps/web/src/components/coach/coach-nav.ts` (`NAV_MODULES`), rendered by `apps/web/src/components/coach/CoachSidebar.tsx` (desktop = vertical sidebar; mobile = horizontal-scroll bottom bar). `getVisibleNavItems(ctx)` is the pure filter; `splitNavItems` partitions into `core` vs `modules` (entitlement-gated).

Top-level items today (standalone coach, in registry order):

| key | href | label / short | contexts | entitlement |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| dashboard | /coach/dashboard | Dashboard / Inicio | all | — |
| clients | /coach/clients | Alumnos | all | — |
| team | /coach/team | Equipo / Team | coach_team only | — |
| programs | /coach/workout-programs | Programas / Planes | all | — |
| **exercises** | **/coach/exercises** | **Ejercicios / Ejer.** | **all** | **—** |
| nutrition | /coach/nutrition-plans | Nutrición / Nutri | all | — |
| brand | /coach/settings | **Mi Marca** / Marca | coach_standalone | — |

Key observations relevant to the consolidation goal:
- **Standalone coach sees ~9-11 flat top-level menus** (dashboard, alumnos, programas, ejercicios, nutrición, Mi Marca, suscripción, soporte, + cardio/movimiento if entitled). This is exactly the "menu clutter" the founder describes — on mobile they all live in one horizontal-scroll bar (`CoachSidebar` lines 263-306, with left/right scroll chevrons because items overflow).
- The menu labeled **"Opciones"** ALREADY EXISTS but only in **team** context (`settings_team`, same href `/coach/settings`). In **standalone** the same page is labeled **"Mi Marca"** (`brand`). So `/coach/settings/page.tsx` is already a context-aware hub titled "Opciones" — but in standalone it renders the branding form inline rather than presenting it as one of three big buttons.
- `exercises` (`/coach/exercises`) is its own top-level menu in ALL contexts — the consolidation target. `programs` (`/coach/workout-programs`) is adjacent in the registry. Moving Ejercicios into the Programas list page = removing the `exercises` entry from `NAV_MODULES` and adding a colored "Lista de ejercicios" button inside `WorkoutProgramsClient` (shell at `apps/web/src/app/coach/workout-programs/WorkoutProgramsClientShell.tsx` → `WorkoutProgramsClient`). Note `coach-nav.test.ts` and E2E specs assert nav titles, so removing an entry touches those tests.

## 2. The `/coach/settings` hub today (`apps/web/src/app/coach/settings/page.tsx`)

Already structured as a hub, context-branched:
- **team_managed**: renders as a clean hub — three big card-buttons (Módulos del equipo, Áreas del builder, Mi Equipo) + DangerZone. This is the visual template the founder wants for the new "Opciones" hub.
- **standalone without branding capability** (free tier): renders a "Mi Marca" upsell wall.
- **standalone with branding**: renders title "Personaliza la app de tus alumnos" + two card-links (Módulos → `/coach/settings/modules`, Áreas del builder → `/coach/settings/areas`) + **branding form inline** (LogoUploadForm + BrandSettingsForm) + DangerZone.

Settings sub-pages that exist:
- `/coach/settings` (hub + branding inline)
- `/coach/settings/modules` — read-only module catalog (ModulesForm)
- `/coach/settings/areas` — builder areas manager
- `/coach/settings/preview` — student dashboard preview

Page `<title>` is already **"Opciones | EVA"** (metadata, line 14-16). So the "Opciones hub with 3 big buttons (Mi Marca / Suscripción / Opciones Coach)" is a refactor of this existing page: today branding is inline and Suscripción is a separate top-level menu; the proposal pulls branding out into its own button and adds the NEW "Opciones Coach" button hosting the Funciones zone.

## 3. How module enabling/disabling works TODAY (entitlement layer)

There are **three independent layers**, all server-side authoritative:

**A. `enabled_modules` (jsonb on `coaches` / `teams`)** — the entitlement flag map.
- Keys: `MODULE_KEYS = ['cardio','movement_assessment','body_composition','nutrition_exchanges']` (`entitlements.service.ts`). Mirrored in `@eva/module-catalog` (`MODULE_CATALOG_KEYS`) — cross-checked by `catalog.test.ts`.
- Resolution: `hasModule(db, key, ctx)` — **team context wins** (pool's `teams.enabled_modules`); else `coaches.enabled_modules`. `assertModule` is the throwing guard at the top of module RSC/actions. Default OFF (`{}`).
- **Compra-only**: `coaches.enabled_modules` is write-only by service-role (column-level grant REVOKE per CLAUDE.md). The coach CANNOT self-toggle. Settings > Módulos is a **read-only catalog** (`ModulesForm.tsx` — no switches, just Activo/Disponible badges + pitch + CTA).
- Written via two write-through paths into `coach_addons` (trigger D1 `sync_coach_enabled_modules` recomputes the jsonb): self-service purchase (webhook) or CEO `admin_grant` (`syncAdminGrants` in `addons.service.ts`).

**B. `coach_addons` (billing source of truth)** — `addons.service.ts`. `source` = `self_service` (paid) | `admin_grant` (CEO courtesy, price 0). The trigger keeps `enabled_modules` in sync. This is the money/entitlement engine; it is NOT a visibility preference.

**C. `EVA_DISABLED_MODULES` (operator kill-switch)** — `isModuleKilledByOperator(key)` / `applyOperatorKillSwitch`. Platform-wide CSV env var, above tenant entitlement, requires redeploy. `hasModule` returns false if killed. UI shows "Temporalmente en mantenimiento" (ModulesForm line 79-84) but doesn't hide the toggle.

**Effective rule today:** `nav-visible = entitlement(enabled_modules) ON AND NOT killed`. There is currently **NO coach-preference layer** — if a module is entitled it shows; the coach can't choose to hide it.

## 4. Base vs paid

**Base (tier-gated, `@eva/tiers` `TIER_CAPABILITIES`):**
- free: no nutrition, no branding (upgrade drivers).
- starter ($19.990): branding, custom exercises, import — NO nutrition.
- pro ($29.990) / elite ($44.990): + nutrition.
- Core features (rutinas, ejercicios, programas, check-in, dashboard) are in every tier.

**Paid add-ons (`ADDON_CONFIG`, $9.990/mo uniform, the 4 MODULE_KEYS):** cardio, movement_assessment, body_composition, nutrition_exchanges ("Nutrición Pro"). `nutrition_exchanges` requires a nutrition tier (Pro+). Sold ONLY in two surfaces (anti-harassment): Settings > Módulos catalog + `/coach/subscription#addons`. Self-service purchase is gated by `SELF_SERVICE_ADDONS_ENABLED` (currently OFF in prod → CTA falls back to mailto).

`/coach/subscription/page.tsx` ("Mi Suscripción") hosts: current plan + composite billing breakdown, the add-ons section (`#addons`, Agregar/Quitar/Cortesía EVA states), and the "Cambiar plan" tier grid with combo add-on checkboxes.

## 5. The proposed preferences system — overlaps & gaps vs today

The draft plan (`docs/audits/nutrition-feature-visibility-{design,PLAN}-2026-06.md`) and a **draft migration already on disk** (`supabase/migrations/20260618200000_feature_prefs.sql`, NOT yet applied per plan) introduce a NEW orthogonal layer:

- **New tables** `coach_feature_prefs` / `client_feature_prefs` (generic, keyed by `domain` + `sections` jsonb + `preset`). These are **side tables with normal table-level grants** — they intentionally avoid the column-grant gotcha that makes `coaches`/`clients` compra-only. The migration DROPs two pre-existing empty tables `coach_nutrition_prefs`/`client_nutrition_prefs` (applied by mistake earlier). RLS: coach owns its rows, client/team can read; client override is coach-managed.
- **Composed visibility rule (proposed):** `visible = ENTITLED (billing, layers A/B/C above, server-side) AND ENABLED (coach toggle, new prefs) [AND client override]`. Preference **only narrows**, never widens — it cannot grant an unentitled feature. This is the critical safety boundary that keeps it from colliding with the entitlement layer.

**Overlaps / collision points to be aware of (do not propose code — flagging):**
1. **Two distinct concepts share the word "module/función":** `enabled_modules` = paid ENTITLEMENT (cannot be coach-toggled, lives on `coaches`); the new `feature_prefs` = free coach PREFERENCE (coach-toggled, side table). The plan correctly separates them (prefs only narrow), but the naming risks confusion in UI/code. The new "Funciones" zone toggles prefs, NOT entitlements — Settings > Módulos (the entitlement catalog) stays read-only and separate.
2. **`nutrition_exchanges` is BOTH a paid module (layer A/B) AND a preset row.** The plan handles this: Pro-tagged sections show ON in the "profesional" preset only if entitled, else render "bloqueado con upsell." So the prefs resolver must read `hasModule` for `requiresModule` rows — it consumes the existing entitlement layer, it doesn't replace it.
3. **CASCADE data-loss invariant:** toggling a section OFF must only hide (render), never delete — same `nutrition_meal_logs ON DELETE CASCADE` gotcha already documented for template propagation. The plan explicitly states "apagar = ocultar, nunca borrar."
4. **The nav registry (`coach-nav.ts`) already has an `entitlement` field** but NO `preference` field. The cardio/movement nav items hide on entitlement OFF. A coach-preference to hide an *entitled* base menu (e.g. hide Nutrición even though Pro) would be a NEW filter dimension in `getVisibleNavItems` — currently unsupported. The consolidation (move Ejercicios into Programas) is a separate, simpler change (remove registry entry + add in-page button) that doesn't need the prefs system at all.
5. **Existing `resolvePreferredWorkspace` / context resolution** (`modules.queries.ts`) already establishes team-vs-standalone scoping that the new `resolveFeaturePrefs(coachId, clientId?, domain)` must mirror (team pool prefs vs standalone).

**Standalone coach (the common case), base plan, no modules ON — 8 top-level menus:**
1. Dashboard
2. Alumnos
3. Programas
4. Ejercicios
5. Nutrición
6. Mi Marca (`/coach/settings`)
7. Suscripción (`/coach/subscription`)

### WEB — RESPONSIVE / mobile (same `CoachSidebar`, `md:` breakpoint flips it)
The **exact same 8–10 items** render as a **horizontal-scrolling bottom bar** (`<nav>` is `flex-row flex-nowrap overflow-x-auto` below `md:`). This is the worst spot — see §2.

### MOBILE app (`apps/mobile`) — Expo tabs

But `CoachMobileChrome.tsx` only surfaces **4 primary thumb tabs** + a "Más" overflow:

So the native app already solved the clutter (4 + overflow). **The web responsive nav did NOT** — it dumps all 8–10 into one horizontal scroll strip.

## 2. Where the clutter is WORST — quantified

**WEB responsive bottom bar (phones on the web app) is the worst offender.**

- 8 menus (10 with modules) crammed into a single horizontal-scroll row.
- Each link is clamped `min-w-[3.5rem] max-w-[5.25rem]` (~56–84px). On a 360–390px viewport, ~4–4.5 items fit; the rest are **off-screen behind a scroll** with animated chevron hints (`canScrollLeft`/`canScrollRight` in `CoachSidebar.tsx` lines 54–72, 241–262).
- Net: **half the coach's menus are hidden behind a horizontal scroll** with no grouping, no overflow menu, no priority. Labels are truncated to `shortLabel` ("Ejer.", "Nutri", "Marca", "Plan", "Ayuda").
- This is strictly worse than the native app, which shows 4 clear tabs + a labeled "Más" sheet for the long tail.

**Mobile-app overflow count:** 6 items behind "Más" — functional but a grab-bag (Ejercicios sits next to Suscripción, Check-ins, Mi cuenta, Mi Marca, Soporte with no grouping).

**Cross-surface drift (a clutter source itself):** the two navs disagree.
- Mobile app has a **`check-ins`** tab and a **`perfil` (Mi cuenta)** screen — neither exists as a web top-level menu.
- Mobile app has **no `team` and no toggleable-module (cardio/movement) tabs** — web does.
- Mobile splits account concerns into **3 separate destinations** (settings = Mi Marca, subscription, perfil = Mi cuenta); web has 2 (Mi Marca, Suscripción). The founder's "Opciones hub with 3 buttons" maps almost 1:1 onto consolidating these scattered account/settings destinations.

**A. The founder's "Opciones" hub directly collapses the account tail.** Today the settings/account/billing concerns are spread across up to 4 destinations:

A single "Opciones" entry hosting 3 big buttons (Mi Marca, Suscripción, **Opciones Coach** = new Funciones zone) removes **1 menu from web** (2→1) and **2 from the mobile overflow** (3→1), shrinking the worst-case web bar from 8 to 7 and the mobile "Más" sheet from 6 to ~4.

**B. The "Ejercicios → into Programas" move (the founder's concrete example) is clean and low-risk.**

- `exercises` is a top-level menu in **all three surfaces** (NAV_MODULES line 50; mobile tab `ejercicios`; mobile overflow). Removing it as a top-level entry and exposing it as a differently-colored "Lista de ejercicios" button on the Programas list page:

**C. Make web responsive adopt the native "4 + Más" pattern.** The single highest-impact fix for the *worst* spot: the web bottom bar should stop being an undifferentiated horizontal scroll of 8–10 items and instead show ~4 primary + an overflow/"Más", mirroring `PRIMARY_TABS` in `CoachMobileChrome.tsx`. # Gap Analysis: Nutrition Feature-Visibility Plan vs EVA's Real Code

The plan is fundamentally sound and the model (visible = ENTITLED ∧ ENABLED, preference only narrows) is correct and matches the existing `getNutritionProEnabledForClient` fail-closed pattern. The migration is mostly correct. But there are real gaps, two of them blocking. Below, ordered by severity.

## BLOCKING / correctness

**1. The migration's `client_feature_prefs_client_read` RLS policy is WRONG (`client_id = auth.uid()`).**
Line 61: `USING (client_id = (SELECT auth.uid()))`. In EVA, `clients.id` is NOT the auth user id. The student-side code proves this: `sections.queries.ts` and `exchanges.repository.ts` always query `clients` by a separate id and read `clients.user_id`/membership; `getStudentExchangeBundle` looks up `clients` by `input.clientId` then reads `team_id/org_id`. The existing `coach_feature_prefs_client_read` policy you wrote (lines 32-34) does it correctly — it joins `clients c WHERE c.id = auth.uid()`... but that is ALSO suspect. **You must verify how a logged-in alumno maps to a `clients` row.** Check an existing client-self RLS policy (e.g. `met_client_select`, `bodycomp_student_self_select` in migration `20260613120000`) and copy its exact predicate. If alumno→client is via `clients.user_id = auth.uid()` (not `clients.id = auth.uid()`), BOTH client-read policies are broken and the alumno will silently read zero prefs → every optional section renders OFF for every alumno regardless of coach toggle. This is the single most likely production break.

**2. The `domain` column has no CHECK and no enforcement, but `preset` validation is punted entirely to "the app".**
`preset text` (nullable, no check) and `domain text` (no check). That's a deliberate generic-storage choice and OK — BUT the design doc (§7b) specified a CHECK constraint `preset in ('basico','intermedio','profesional')` and the plan dropped it for genericity. Fine, except the resolver MUST then treat an unknown/null preset as `'basico'` (the safe default), and the write-path server action MUST validate with Zod. Make that explicit in F0/F1 or you get garbage presets that the resolver `s.presets[coachPrefs.preset]` indexes as `undefined` → section silently OFF. Low effort, must not be skipped.

## HIGH / design mismatch with real code

**3. "New Opciones hub with 3 big buttons" — `/coach/settings` is ALREADY titled "Opciones" and is already a card-hub (in team mode).**
`coach/settings/page.tsx` metadata title = `'Opciones'`; the `coach-nav.ts` entry `settings_team` already labels `/coach/settings` as "Opciones". In team mode the page is already exactly the "hub with cards" the plan wants (Módulos / Áreas / Mi Equipo cards). The plan describes building a NEW hub as if greenfield. **Correction:** this is a restructure of the existing `/coach/settings`, not a new route. "Mi Marca" is the *standalone* `/coach/settings` body (BrandSettingsForm), and "Suscripción" is `/coach/subscription` (nav key `billing`). The plan must (a) decide whether "Opciones" becomes the hub and branding moves to `/coach/settings/brand`, or branding stays and the hub is a new parent — and reconcile with the fact that the nav already routes both `brand` and `settings_team` to `/coach/settings` context-aware. There's a naming clash: the plan's "Opciones Coach" button vs the page already called "Opciones". Pick distinct names.

**4. There is already a module-toggle UI at `/coach/settings/modules` (`ModulesForm`) — the plan ignores it.**
The "Funciones" zone the plan invents overlaps conceptually with the existing read-only Módulos catalog page (`getModulesContext` + `ModulesForm`, driven by `MODULE_CATALOG` from `@eva/module-catalog`). The plan should explicitly state the relationship: Módulos = ENTITLEMENT (read-only, billing, "what you've bought"); Funciones = PREFERENCE (toggles, "what you show"). They're adjacent and a coach will confuse them. Recommend they live as two zones in the same Opciones Coach page with a one-line explainer, and reuse the `MODULE_CATALOG` copy for the Pro badges/upsell rather than re-authoring strings.

**5. The "move Ejercicios into Programas" consolidation is under-specified and collides with the entitlement model.**
The nav is a typed registry (`NAV_MODULES` in `coach-nav.ts`), pure and unit-tested, with E2E specs that assert nav titles (`collectNavTitles`, `aside nav a[title]` — see the divider comment lines 285-287). Removing the `exercises` entry will break those E2E specs and any test asserting the title "Ejercicios". `/coach/exercises/page.tsx` is also context-aware (team pool vs standalone, `canCreateExercises`). The plan says "move it into the Programas list page as a colored button" but: (a) `/coach/exercises` must remain a reachable route (deep links, the client app `/c/[coach_slug]/exercises` exists too); (b) the consolidation needs to go through `NAV_MODULES` (remove/flag the entry) + update `coach-nav` tests, not just drop a button. Crucially: **should "Ejercicios" be a Funciones toggle too?** The founder goal (2) says coach prefs over base functions including nav. If yes, the nav registry needs a `featurePref` hook parallel to the existing `entitlement` hook — that's a real addition the plan doesn't mention. Right now `getVisibleNavItems` only knows `entitlement`, not preference.

**6. Resolver signature omits the operator kill-switch and the team/pool entitlement path.**
The design's `resolveNutritionPrefs(coachId, clientId?)` reduces entitlement to `resolveProEntitlement(coachId)`. But the real entitlement (`hasExchangesModuleForClientContext`) resolves by **resource context** (pool wins via `clientTeamId`, then plan coach), and `hasModule`/`applyOperatorKillSwitch` apply `isModuleKilledByOperator`. The plan's model line in the PLAN doc *does* mention `NOT killed`, but the resolver pseudocode in the design doc does not thread teamId/orgId or the kill-switch. **Correction:** the resolver must take the same resource context the existing code uses (planId or {coachId, clientTeamId, clientOrgId}), not just coachId — otherwise team-pool alumnos get wrong Pro gating. Reuse `findPlanModuleContext` + `hasExchangesModuleForClientContext` verbatim; don't reimplement.

## MEDIUM

**7. `updated_at` trigger function naming is sloppy for a "generic" system.**
The function is `public.nutrition_set_updated_at()` but the tables are generic `*_feature_prefs`. Cosmetic, but it contradicts the genericity thesis and will confuse the next dev. Rename to `feature_prefs_set_updated_at` (or reuse an existing generic `set_updated_at` if one exists — grep before adding).

**8. The "drop the empty `coach_nutrition_prefs`/`client_nutrition_prefs`" reconciliation is unconditional DROP.**
Lines 6-7 `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS` with no guard that they're empty. The plan asserts they're empty ("aplicadas por error"), but `merge_branch`/forward-only re-runs ALL history (per CLAUDE.md). If those tables ever held data this silently destroys it. Low real risk (you say empty) but add a defensive comment and confirm via `list_tables` they have 0 rows before this ships. Also confirm no other migration or `database.types.ts` consumer references the old table names (grep showed `database.types.ts` references the pool functions, not these tables — but regen types after).

**9. CASCADE-on-meal-logs gotcha: correctly identified, but add a guardrail to the spec.**
The plan correctly says toggle = render-only, never delete. Good. The risk isn't the toggle itself — it's a future dev wiring "disable section" to a data mutation. Add to the governance rule: a Funcion toggle writes ONLY to `*_feature_prefs.sections` jsonb; it is forbidden to delete/null any `nutrition_*` row. Make it a PR-review checklist item, not just prose.

**10. No index on `(domain)` lookups, and PK is `(coach_id, domain)`.**
Resolver always queries `WHERE coach_id = ? AND domain = ?` → PK covers it, fine. But the alumno-read and team-read policies do `coach_id IN (SELECT current_user_pool_coach_ids())` and an EXISTS subquery on `clients` — verify these don't add `multiple_permissive_policies` perf warnings (you have THREE SELECT policies on `coach_feature_prefs`: owner_all FOR ALL, client_read, team_read — `FOR ALL` already includes SELECT, so it stacks with the two SELECT-only policies → permissive-policy multiplication, exactly the perf issue your DB audit fought down from 575→510). Run `get_advisors` after apply; consider consolidating into fewer policies.

## What the plan got RIGHT (keep)
- Side-table approach avoids the `coaches`/`clients` column-grant gotcha entirely — correct, and it does need its own `GRANT ... TO authenticated` which the migration has (lines 23, 48).
- Pure `FeatureSections` config mirroring `@eva/module-catalog` is the right pattern and matches `MODULE_CATALOG_KEYS`/`MODULE_KEYS` cross-check discipline (add the same `catalog.test.ts`-style test).
- Entitlement stays relational (`coach_addons`/`enabled_modules`), preference goes to jsonb — correct, matches the trigger-D1 compra-only invariant.
- Pro = `nutrition_exchanges` (= "Nutrición Pro" in catalog) is the correct mapping; `requiresModule` typed as `ModuleKey` works.
- Merge-over-defaults for missing jsonb keys handles the "old blob lacks new section" case.

## Concrete corrections checklist
1. Fix both `*_client_read` policies to use EVA's real alumno→`clients` predicate (verify against `bodycomp_student_self_select`); likely `clients.user_id = auth.uid()`, NOT `clients.id = auth.uid()`.
2. Resolver takes resource context (planId or team/org/coach), reuses `findPlanModuleContext` + `hasExchangesModuleForClientContext` + kill-switch — not bare `coachId`.
3. Treat null/unknown `preset` as `'basico'`; Zod-validate preset+domain in the write action.
4. Reframe "new Opciones hub" as a restructure of the existing `/coach/settings` (already "Opciones") + `/coach/subscription`; resolve the name clash; relate Funciones (preference) to the existing `/coach/settings/modules` (entitlement, `ModulesForm`).
5. Nav consolidation goes through `NAV_MODULES` registry + its unit tests + the nav-title E2E specs; keep `/coach/exercises` route alive; if Ejercicios is to be coach-toggleable, add a `featurePref` hook to `coach-nav` parallel to `entitlement`.
6. Rename `nutrition_set_updated_at` → generic; consolidate the 3 permissive SELECT policies; run `get_advisors`; confirm old tables empty before DROP; regen `database.types.ts`.

roles.verdict: APPROVE WITH CHANGES. The architecture is sound and notably well-aligned with EVA's existing patterns: the ENTITLED ∧ ENABLED model correctly generalizes the existing fail-closed getNutritionProEnabledForClient, the side-table approach correctly sidesteps the coaches/clients column-grant gotcha, and JSONB-for-preferences / relational-for-entitlement is the right split. The draft migration's RLS is actually CORRECT (one audit agent's "BLOCKING" client-read concern was based on a wrong assumption — verified that EVA's convention is clients.id = auth.uid() everywhere, which both *_client_read policies use correctly). The blocking issues are smaller and concrete: a permissive-policy stack that will trip your DB perf advisors, an unguarded resolver context that breaks team-pool alumnos, the absent-row grandfathering trap, and naming/scope drift between the plan and the code that already exists. None are fatal; all are fixable before build.

topConcerns.title: Resolver signature drops resource context → team-pool alumnos get wrong Pro gating
topConcerns.detail: The design's resolveNutritionPrefs(coachId, clientId?) reduces entitlement to resolveProEntitlement(coachId). But the REAL entitlement resolves by resource context: hasExchangesModuleForClientContext + findPlanModuleContext (exchanges.repository.ts) where the TEAM POOL wins via clientTeamId, then plan coach, and hasModule applies isModuleKilledByOperator (EVA_DISABLED_MODULES). A coachId-only resolver will mis-gate every team-pool alumno (Movida is a live team) and silently bypass the operator kill-switch. The resolver MUST take the same context the existing code uses (planId or {coachId, clientTeamId, clientOrgId}) and REUSE hasExchangesModuleForClientContext verbatim — do not reimplement entitlement. F0 must call the existing entitlement path, not a new resolveProEntitlement.

topConcerns.title: Absent prefs row + new-section default = silent behavior change for existing coaches
topConcerns.detail: This is the #1 'don't break current coaches' risk. Every current coach has NO row in coach_feature_prefs on day one. If the resolver treats absent preset as undefined, s.presets[undefined] is undefined → every optional section renders OFF. That is the INTENDED end state (advanced OFF by default) — but it is a behavior change applied to live coaches who today SEE micros base / plate / off-plan etc. because there is no gate. Industry rule (GitLab MR 122467, fly.io): a column/blob default does NOT grandfather anyone — you must decide explicitly. Two safe moves: (1) resolver treats null/unknown preset as 'basico' deterministically (never undefined-index); (2) decide per-section whether currently-visible-to-all sections should grandfather ON for existing coaches (backfill 'intermedio' or seed rows) vs. ship Basico and accept the visible reduction. The plan says 'Default Básico para coaches existentes' but does NOT acknowledge that this HIDES sections those coaches' alumnos currently see. Make it a conscious, announced decision, not an accident.

topConcerns.title: coach_feature_prefs has 3 stacked permissive policies (owner_all FOR ALL + 2 SELECT) → multiple_permissive_policies advisor regression
topConcerns.detail: The migration creates owner_all (FOR ALL, which includes SELECT), client_read (SELECT), and team_read (SELECT) on coach_feature_prefs. For an authenticated SELECT, Postgres ORs all three permissive policies → exactly the multiple_permissive_policies perf issue your DB audit fought down 575→510 (project_db_audit_20260616). On a hot read path (every nutrition render resolves prefs) this matters. Consolidate: make owner_all FOR ALL but scope the SELECT-visibility into a single combined SELECT policy, OR split owner into INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE + one unified SELECT policy (owner OR client OR pool). Run get_advisors after apply and confirm 0 new multiple_permissive warnings before merge. Same review for client_feature_prefs (coach_all FOR ALL + client_read SELECT + team_all FOR ALL stack).

topConcerns.title: Plan describes the Opciones hub as greenfield, but /coach/settings is ALREADY titled 'Opciones' and is already a card-hub
topConcerns.detail: coach/settings/page.tsx metadata title = 'Opciones'; the nav key settings_team already labels /coach/settings 'Opciones' and renders a 3-card hub in team mode. The standalone nav routes BOTH brand ('Mi Marca') and settings_team ('Opciones') to /coach/settings context-aware. The plan's 'Opciones Coach' button name COLLIDES with the page already called 'Opciones', and the 'new hub' is really a restructure of the existing settings page + pulling branding out of its inline body. Also /coach/settings/modules (ModulesForm) already exists as the read-only ENTITLEMENT catalog — the new 'Funciones' PREFERENCE zone is adjacent and coaches WILL confuse 'Módulos' (what you bought) with 'Funciones' (what you show). Reframe F1 as a restructure, pick non-colliding names, and write the one-line explainer distinguishing entitlement vs preference. Don't build a parallel hub next to the one that exists.

topConcerns.title: Nav consolidation (Ejercicios→Programas) under-specified and untested against existing nav tests
topConcerns.detail: NAV_MODULES (coach-nav.ts) is pure and unit-tested (coach-nav.test.ts) and E2E specs assert nav titles via aside nav a[title]. Removing the exercises entry breaks those tests — the plan must update them, not just drop the entry. topConcerns.title: Mobile clutter (the founder's actual goal #1) is barely addressed by the plan
topConcerns.detail: The audit is explicit: the WORST clutter is the WEB responsive bottom bar — 8-10 items in one flat horizontal scroll, half off-screen, no overflow/'More'. improvements.title: Rename the generic trigger function; it's named nutrition_set_updated_at on generic tables
improvements.detail: The migration's trigger fn is public.nutrition_set_updated_at() but the tables are generic *_feature_prefs. Rename to feature_prefs_set_updated_at (or grep for an existing generic set_updated_at and reuse it). Cosmetic but it contradicts the genericity thesis and will mislead the next dev who greps 'nutrition'.

improvements.title: Guard the DROP TABLE of the empty scaffolding tables
improvements.detail: Lines 6-7 DROP IF EXISTS coach_nutrition_prefs/client_nutrition_prefs unconditionally. The plan asserts they're empty — CONFIRM 0 rows via list_tables/execute_sql before this ships (merge_branch replays ALL history; an unconditional DROP on a table that ever held data is destructive). Add a comment documenting they were empty scaffolding. Regenerate database.types.ts after and grep that nothing references the old table names.

improvements.title: Adopt the native '4 primary + Más' pattern for the web responsive bottom bar
improvements.detail: Independently of prefs: mirror CoachMobileChrome.PRIMARY_TABS in CoachSidebar's <md bottom bar. After folding Ejercicios into Programas and Marca/Suscripción into Opciones, web mobile primaries become ~Inicio·Alumnos·Planes·Nutri·Más, with the long tail (Soporte, Opciones, entitled modules) behind Más. This is the single highest-leverage fix for founder goal #1 and is decoupled from the prefs backend.

improvements.title: Gate dependent dashboard widgets together with their section, not just the tab
improvements.detail: Cross-feature dependency pitfall (from the B2B2C research): MacroBar / ComplianceRing / NutritionDailySummary consume nutrition. When a section is toggled OFF, the resolver flags must also gate these dependent widgets on dashboard.service / heroComplianceBundle — not only the nutrition tab — or you get a hidden section whose macro ring still renders. The single resolver should be read wherever any nutrition-derived widget mounts.

roles.biggestRisk: Silently changing what EXISTING coaches' alumnos see. Today there is no preference gate, so optional sections (micros base, plate method, off-plan log, notes) are visible to everyone who has a plan. The moment F2 wires the resolver and existing coaches default to 'Basico' (or to an absent/undefined preset), those sections vanish from live alumno apps mid-relationship — the exact 'recompute ciego = sorpresa' / 'new column default does not grandfather' trap the research flags as the top migration failure. This is a backend/data-default decision disguised as a UI default, it hits real paying coaches (and the live Movida team pool), and it is invisible in testing because fresh test accounts have no prior 'everything visible' baseline to lose. Mitigation: make grandfathering an explicit, announced decision with a backfill, and never let the resolver index a preset by undefined.
missing: A grandfathering / backfill strategy for the ~live coaches who today see all sections ungated — the plan's 'Default Básico para existentes' silently reduces what their alumnos see; needs an explicit retroactive-vs-new-only decision and a separate batched idempotent backfill migration.
The web responsive bottom-bar overflow ('4 primary + Más') redesign — the founder's #1 goal (mobile clutter) and the worst-clutter spot per the audit is not in any phase.

A featurePref hook in getVisibleNavItems / coach-nav.ts — founder goal (2) says prefs over base functions INCLUDING nav, but the nav registry only supports entitlement gating today; making nav items coach-toggleable is unscoped.
Updates to coach-nav.test.ts and the nav-title E2E specs that will break when the exercises entry is removed — not mentioned in the consolidation step.

Cross-surface drift with apps/mobile: the Expo coach tabs (ejercicios tab, NAV_META) and any mobile reading of nutrition sections must consume the same resolver/registry or web and native will diverge; the plan is web-only.

Reconciliation of the new 'Funciones' (preference) zone with the existing read-only /coach/settings/modules 'Módulos' (entitlement) page — they are adjacent and confusable; needs explicit IA + copy distinguishing 'bought' vs 'shown'.
Rename of the nutrition_set_updated_at trigger fn and a confirm-empty guard on the DROP TABLE of the scaffolding tables + database.types.ts regen.
get_advisors run + permissive-policy consolidation as an explicit DoD item on the migration, consistent with the standing DB audit discipline.

Empty-state guidance: when a section is ON-but-no-data (e.g. plan exists, no logs yet) the alumno should get a guided empty state, not a blank — distinct from OFF (hidden). The plan handles hide vs show but not show-but-empty (NN/g pitfall).

roles.verdict: Conditional GO. The architecture is sound and the entitlement/preference separation is correct, but the draft migration ships with real correctness bugs (team/pool read gap, FOR ALL + SELECT policy stacking, generic-vs-nutrition naming, missing domain CHECK) and the resolver as specced reduces entitlement context to coachId, which is wrong for pool alumnos. Fix the migration and resolver signature before any apply. The single most-cited "BLOCKING" item in the upstream audit JSON (clients.id != auth.uid()) is FALSE for EVA — clients.id IS the legacy auth identity (confirmed in 20260608180000 + 20260613120000), so do NOT "fix" those predicates.

topConcerns.title: Team/pool alumno cannot read its coach's feature prefs (silent OFF for all optional sections)
topConcerns.detail: coach_feature_prefs_client_read uses `clients c WHERE c.id = auth.uid() AND c.coach_id = coach_feature_prefs.coach_id`. In a TEAM, the alumno's clients.coach_id is the assigned coach, but the NUTRITION PLAN (and thus the prefs that should drive their view) may belong to a DIFFERENT pool coach — current_user_pool_coach_ids() exists precisely because plan-author != clients.coach_id in pools. There is NO client-side pool read policy on coach_feature_prefs (the team_read policy is for COACHES reading peers, not for the alumno). Result: a pooled alumno reads zero coach prefs -> resolver falls to defaults -> every optional section renders OFF regardless of the coach's toggle. This is the exact 'breaks current coaches' failure mode. Add a client-pool read branch keyed off the alumno's plan/team coach, mirroring how sections.queries resolves entitlement via findPlanModuleContext.

topConcerns.title: Resolver signature resolveFeaturePrefs(coachId, clientId?) drops the entitlement resource-context
topConcerns.detail: The design's resolveProEntitlement(coachId) collapses entitlement to the coach, but the live code (hasExchangesModuleForClientContext + findPlanModuleContext in sections.queries.ts) resolves Pro by RESOURCE context: pool/team wins, then plan coach, then operator kill-switch (isModuleKilledByOperator). If the resolver only takes coachId it will compute wrong Pro gating for pooled alumnos and ignore EVA_DISABLED_MODULES. The resolver MUST thread the same context the existing code uses (planId or {coachId, clientTeamId, clientOrgId}) and reuse findPlanModuleContext/hasExchangesModuleForClientContext verbatim, not reimplement. The PLAN doc text mentions 'NOT killed' but the design pseudocode omits both teamId and the kill-switch.

topConcerns.title: Policy stacking: FOR ALL owner policy + two SELECT policies = multiple_permissive_policies regression
topConcerns.detail: coach_feature_prefs has owner_all (FOR ALL, which includes SELECT) + client_read (SELECT) + team_read (SELECT) = 3 permissive SELECT policies on one table. client_feature_prefs has coach_all (FOR ALL) + client_read (SELECT) + team_all (FOR ALL) similarly stacked. This is exactly the multiple_permissive_policies perf class the DB audit fought down 575->510. Each SELECT evaluates all permissive policies OR'd. Run get_advisors after apply; consider splitting owner_all into explicit INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE so SELECT has fewer overlapping policies, or consolidate the read branches into one policy with OR.

topConcerns.title: No backfill strategy for existing coaches -> default Basico silently hides advanced sections they currently use
topConcerns.detail: Absent prefs row resolves to preset default = Basico = everything optional OFF. The research's highest-confidence finding: a new default does NOT grandfather existing users. Today a Pro coach already sees micros_advanced via getNutritionProEnabledForClient (proEnabled). If F0 ships and the resolver treats 'no row' as Basico, every existing Pro/nutrition coach loses advanced sections overnight with no row to explain it. Decide explicitly: either (a) backfill a 'profesional' (or intermedio) row for coaches with existing nutrition plans/logs, or (b) make the resolver's no-row default usage-derived (has nutrition_plans -> intermedio/profesional). Do NOT rely on the column DEFAULT 'basico'.

improvements.title: Rename the generic trigger function; it is nutrition-named in a generic system
improvements.detail: Migration defines public.nutrition_set_updated_at() but the tables are generic *_feature_prefs. This directly contradicts the genericity thesis and will confuse the next dev wiring 'training'. grep first for an existing generic set_updated_at()/moddatetime; if none, name it public.feature_prefs_set_updated_at(). Cosmetic but cheap and the migration is unapplied so there's no cost to fixing now.

improvements.title: Add a domain guard and treat unknown/null preset as the safe default in the resolver
improvements.detail: domain text and preset text have no CHECK (deliberate for genericity, fine), but the resolver MUST: (1) treat null/unknown preset as 'basico' so s.presets[preset] never indexes undefined -> section silently OFF in a non-deterministic way; (2) Zod-validate preset+domain in the write server action (preset in basico|intermedio|profesional, domain in a known set). Without server-side validation a client can write garbage presets into the jsonb-adjacent column.

improvements.title: Confirm the two dropped tables are empty before shipping the unconditional DROP
improvements.detail: Lines 6-7 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS coach_nutrition_prefs/client_nutrition_prefs with no row guard. merge_branch replays ALL history (forward-only) so this re-runs on every merge. The plan asserts they're empty; verify via list_tables/execute_sql (SELECT count(*)) before apply, and grep database.types.ts + repos for any reference to the old names. Regen database.types.ts after apply (CLAUDE.md: apply_migration does not write the local .sql or types).

improvements.title: Make the 'disable = hide never delete' invariant a DB-enforced/PR-checklist rule, not just prose
improvements.detail: The CASCADE risk (nutrition_meal_logs.meal_id ON DELETE CASCADE) is correctly identified, but the real risk is a future dev wiring a section toggle to a data mutation. Codify: a Funciones toggle writes ONLY to *_feature_prefs.sections jsonb; it is forbidden to delete/null any nutrition_* row. Add it to the governance rule and the PR template, same weight as 'every new section gets a FeatureSections row'.

improvements.title: Add resolver unit tests that pin the AND-narrow invariant and the pool path
improvements.detail: Mirror the catalog.test.ts discipline: (1) core sections always true regardless of prefs; (2) preference can only narrow — entitled=false + wants=true => false; (3) wants=false + entitled=true => false; (4) pool alumno resolves via plan-coach prefs not clients.coach_id; (5) kill-switch (EVA_DISABLED_MODULES) forces false even when entitled+wanted. These are the security-relevant cases and they are pure-function testable.

improvements.title: Index/PK check is fine but verify the client_read EXISTS subquery doesn't go per-row
improvements.detail: PK(coach_id,domain) covers the resolver's WHERE coach_id=? AND domain=? — good. But coach_feature_prefs_client_read does an EXISTS on clients per the SELECT; ensure it's InitPlan-shaped (wrap auth.uid() in (SELECT auth.uid())) — it is in the draft. Just run EXPLAIN with loops=1 in a tx-rollback against the real shape before merge, per the 2026-06-09 incident rule.

roles.biggestRisk: Silent regression for existing nutrition/Pro coaches: the combination of (a) no backfill (no-row -> Basico) and (b) the team/pool client-read gap means that on the day F0 ships, real coaches who currently see micros/advanced sections — and their alumnos in pools — lose those sections with no error, no row, and no obvious cause. It will look like 'nutrition broke' rather than a config default, and because preference only narrows and re-checks server-side, there's no client-side tell. This is the highest-likelihood way the plan violates the founder's hard constraint 'MUST NOT break things current coaches rely on'.
missing: No migration backfill for existing coaches (the single biggest 'don't break current coaches' gap) — column DEFAULT does not grandfather anyone
No pool/team client-read policy for coach_feature_prefs — pooled alumnos can't read the prefs that drive their view
Resolver does not consume operator kill-switch (EVA_DISABLED_MODULES / isModuleKilledByOperator) or team/org resource context — only coachId

No server-action write-path spec: Zod validation of preset/domain, and the GRANT on the side table is table-level (correct, no column-grant gotcha) but the write action's auth boundary (coach can only write own row; client_feature_prefs only the managing coach) needs explicit tests
No get_advisors run planned post-apply despite 3 stacked permissive SELECT policies (perf regression class the DB audit specifically reduced)
database.types.ts regeneration + grep for old table-name references not called out as a step

No statement on what happens to the mobile app (apps/mobile speaks PostgREST directly) — it will read these tables too; the RLS gap and the resolver logic must be mirrored or the mobile alumno view diverges from web

No decision on whether 'Ejercicios in nav' becomes a feature_pref toggle — getVisibleNavItems only knows entitlement, not preference; adding a nav featurePref hook is unscoped work the founder's goal (2) implies

roles.verdict: APPROVE WITH CHANGES. The model (visible = ENTITLED AND NOT killed AND ENABLED, preference only narrows, resolved server-side) is correct and matches EVA's existing fail-closed getNutritionProEnabledForClient pattern. The side-table storage choice correctly sidesteps the coaches/clients column-grant gotcha. The plan is buildable as written for nutrition. But three things must be locked before code: (1) the per-coach DEFAULT-resolution for EXISTING coaches/alumnos (the real "don't break current coaches" trap — empty prefs row must not silently hide things people already see), (2) the nav consolidation (Ejercicios -> Programas, new Opciones hub) is under-specified versus the typed NAV_MODULES registry + its tests + mobile bottom-bar clutter, and (3) the dependent-widget gating on the dashboard. I also correct a false-alarm: the migration's client-read RLS is actually CORRECT for EVA.

topConcerns.title: Existing coaches/alumnos with NO prefs row must keep what they see today (default-resolution is the real break risk)
topConcerns.detail: The render path is `wants = clientPrefs?.[key] ?? coachPrefs.sections[key] ?? s.presets[coachPrefs.preset]`. For every coach/alumno that exists TODAY there is NO row in coach_feature_prefs, so coachPrefs is undefined and `coachPrefs.preset` throws OR resolves to undefined -> `s.presets[undefined]` = undefined -> section silently OFF. Today an entitled coach with Nutricion Pro SEES micros_advanced (proEnabled in sections.queries.ts). Ship this naively and on deploy every existing Pro coach loses advanced micros, plate, off-plan log, etc., with zero action on their part. The research bundle's highest-confidence finding applies verbatim: a column/blob default does NOT grandfather existing rows. Two non-negotiables: (a) the resolver MUST treat missing coachPrefs as a safe default object {preset:'basico'} BUT (b) 'basico' hides everything optional, which is exactly the regression. So the F1 migration MUST backfill: for each existing coach derive a preset from observed usage (has nutrition_meal_logs / micros set / exchanges module -> 'profesional' or at least enable the sections they already use), else 'intermedio'/'basico'. Frontend resolver and backfill must be designed together, in the same phase, not deferred.

topConcerns.title: Dependent dashboard widgets must gate WITH the section, or you ship inconsistent UI
topConcerns.detail: The B2B2C research flags this and it is live in EVA's current diff: MacroBar.tsx, ComplianceRing.tsx, NutritionDailySummary.tsx, MacroRingSummary.tsx, heroComplianceBundle.ts all render nutrition-derived UI on the coach dashboard and the alumno home — they do NOT go through NutritionShell/NutritionTabB5. If a coach disables a nutrition section (or the whole domain) but the dashboard hero ring still renders macro adherence, the alumno sees a 'broken/loading' surface (NN/g empty-state pitfall). The plan only names NutritionTabB5 + NutritionShell + sections.queries.ts as wiring targets. resolveFeaturePrefs MUST also be consumed by the dashboard bundle (heroComplianceBundle.ts / dashboard.service.ts), and on-but-empty surfaces need guided empty states, never blanks.

topConcerns.title: Nav consolidation (Ejercicios->Programas + Opciones hub) is under-specified vs the typed registry, its tests, and the mobile bottom bar
topConcerns.detail: NAV_MODULES in coach-nav.ts is pure, unit-tested (coach-nav.test.ts) and asserted by E2E nav-title specs. Dropping the `exercises` entry breaks those tests; they must be updated in the same change. Keep the /coach/exercises ROUTE alive (deep links + the alumno /c/[slug]/exercises exists). The 'Lista de ejercicios' button belongs in LibraryHeader.tsx on the Programas page as variant='outline' (different color). Separately: the founder's #1 stated pain is mobile clutter, and CoachSidebar.tsx renders the SAME 8-10 items as a flat horizontal-scroll bottom bar with ~half off-screen. The plan does not touch this. The native app already solved it (PRIMARY_TABS=4 + 'Mas' overflow in CoachMobileChrome.tsx). Web responsive should adopt the same 4-primary + Mas pattern via splitNavItems, or the consolidation will not visibly reduce clutter where it hurts most.

topConcerns.title: 'Opciones' hub already exists and the name clashes — this is a restructure, not greenfield
topConcerns.detail: /coach/settings already has metadata title 'Opciones' and the team_managed branch already renders a 3-card hub. The nav routes BOTH `brand` (Mi Marca, standalone) and `settings_team` (Opciones, team) to /coach/settings context-aware. So the new hub is a refactor of /coach/settings + /coach/subscription, and the founder's third button 'Opciones Coach' collides with the page already named 'Opciones'. Pick distinct names (e.g. hub='Opciones', third card='Funciones'/'Preferencias'). Also relate the new 'Funciones' zone to the EXISTING read-only /coach/settings/modules (ModulesForm): Modulos = ENTITLEMENT (what you bought, read-only), Funciones = PREFERENCE (what you show). A coach will confuse them without a one-line explainer; reuse MODULE_CATALOG copy for the Pro badges/upsell.

topConcerns.title: goals_bodycomp section maps to the wrong module gate
topConcerns.detail: The PLAN table marks both 'Micros avanzados' AND 'Objetivos por composicion corporal' as requiresModule = nutrition_exchanges. But composition-corporal objectives conceptually belong to the body_composition module (separate paid add-on, separate entitlement path). Resolver must read hasModule('body_composition') for goals_bodycomp, not 'nutrition_exchanges' — otherwise a coach who bought body_composition but not Nutricion Pro gets the section incorrectly locked, and vice versa. requiresModule is per-section; confirm each row's module key against MODULE_KEYS (cardio, movement_assessment, body_composition, nutrition_exchanges).

topConcerns.title: Resolver signature drops team/org context and the operator kill-switch
topConcerns.detail: The design pseudocode resolves entitlement from bare coachId via resolveProEntitlement(coachId). But real entitlement (hasExchangesModuleForClientContext) resolves by RESOURCE context where the team pool's enabled_modules WINS over the plan coach, and hasModule applies isModuleKilledByOperator (EVA_DISABLED_MODULES). The PLAN's model line says 'AND NOT killed' but the resolver code omits both. Reuse findPlanModuleContext + hasExchangesModuleForClientContext + the kill-switch verbatim; pass the resource context (planId or {coachId, clientTeamId, clientOrgId}), not just coachId, or team-pool alumnos get wrong Pro gating.

improvements.title: Correction: the migration's client-read RLS is CORRECT for EVA — do not 'fix' it
improvements.detail: A sibling auditor flagged coach_feature_prefs_client_read (c.id = auth.uid()) and client_feature_prefs_client_read (client_id = auth.uid()) as wrong, assuming alumno maps via clients.user_id. Verified against the schema: the `clients` table has NO user_id column; clients.id IS the auth uid. The established pattern (20260608180000 foods/exercises consolidation, 20260608230000 enterprise alumno context, team_foundation) all use `clients c WHERE c.id = auth.uid()`. The migration as written is consistent and correct. Changing it to user_id would BREAK alumno reads (every section OFF). Keep as-is.

improvements.title: Make absent-prefs the explicit safe default in the resolver, with usage-derived backfill
improvements.detail: Resolver: const coachPrefs = (await getCoachFeaturePrefs(coachId, domain)) ?? { preset: 'basico', sections: {} }; treat unknown/null preset as 'basico'. Zod-validate preset+domain in the write server action (preset in basico|intermedio|profesional). Then a one-time, batched, idempotent (forward-only, replay-safe per merge_branch) backfill INSERT into coach_feature_prefs deriving preset from signals (has nutrition_meal_logs OR nutrition_exchanges entitlement -> profesional; has any nutrition plan -> intermedio; else basico). This is the single change that makes 'don't break current coaches' true.

improvements.title: Add a domain-level master toggle and decide its dashboard cascade now
improvements.detail: Founder goal (2) is 'enable/disable modules AND functions, including base functions'. The plan models per-section nutrition flags but not 'hide Nutricion entirely for this coach/alumno'. getVisibleNavItems in coach-nav.ts only knows `entitlement`, not preference. Add a parallel `featurePref` hook to the registry so a coach can hide an ENTITLED base menu (Nutricion, and later Ejercicios). When the domain is OFF: drop the nav entry AND the dependent dashboard widgets (MacroBar/ComplianceRing/hero bundle) together — gate them in heroComplianceBundle.ts off the same resolver.

improvements.title: Cosmetic/correctness migration nits to clean before apply
improvements.detail: (a) Rename nutrition_set_updated_at -> feature_prefs_set_updated_at (it triggers generic *_feature_prefs tables, not nutrition) — grep for an existing generic set_updated_at first and reuse. (b) The pool helpers are called unqualified (current_user_pool_client_ids()) vs schema-qualified public.current_user_pool_client_ids() everywhere else; align for safety. (c) coach_feature_prefs has THREE policies (owner_all FOR ALL + client_read SELECT + team_read SELECT) — FOR ALL includes SELECT, so this stacks as multiple_permissive_policies, the exact perf class your DB audit fought from 575->510. Run get_advisors after apply and consider consolidating. (d) Confirm coach_nutrition_prefs/client_nutrition_prefs have 0 rows before the unconditional DROP, and regen database.types.ts after.

improvements.title: Use a contextual entry point for the exercise library, mirror native overflow on web
improvements.detail: June-2026 nav research: nest a supporting tool inside the page it serves (exercise library is consumed WHILE building programs). 'Lista de ejercicios' as an in-page outline button on Programas is the textbook pattern. For the worst clutter spot (web responsive bottom bar) adopt the native 4-primary-tabs + 'Mas' overflow already implemented in CoachMobileChrome.tsx (PRIMARY_TABS), driven by the existing core/modules split from splitNavItems. This is the change that actually delivers the founder's 'consolidate the menu' goal, separate from the prefs system.

improvements.title: Onboarding preset write must never touch enabled_modules
improvements.detail: The onboarding question writes ONLY coach_feature_prefs.preset. Add an explicit governance/PR-review rule: a Funcion toggle or preset write writes ONLY to *_feature_prefs.sections/preset jsonb — it is forbidden to write enabled_modules (trigger D1 would pisar it and/or it would gift paid features) and forbidden to delete/null any nutrition_* row (the nutrition_meal_logs ON DELETE CASCADE data-loss invariant). Make both PR-checklist items, not just prose.

roles.biggestRisk: Silent regression on deploy: existing coaches/alumnos have no prefs row, so the `?? s.presets[coachPrefs.preset]` chain resolves entitled-but-previously-visible sections (advanced micros, plate, off-plan log) to OFF — and on the dashboard the dependent widgets (MacroBar, ComplianceRing, hero bundle) keep rendering, producing broken/empty surfaces. From the frontend lens this looks like 'features disappeared and the dashboard is half-broken' with zero user action, and it hits paying Pro coaches first. The fix (safe-default resolver + usage-derived backfill + gating dependent widgets through the same resolver) must land in the SAME phase as the wiring, not be deferred to F2.
missing: No backfill/grandfathering step for existing coaches and alumnos — the plan treats this as greenfield. Empty prefs row currently degrades to 'everything optional OFF'.

No handling of the dashboard's nutrition-derived widgets (MacroBar, ComplianceRing, NutritionDailySummary, MacroRingSummary, heroComplianceBundle) — they bypass NutritionShell/NutritionTabB5 and will render disabled/empty sections.
No plan for the mobile/responsive bottom-bar clutter, which is the founder's stated #1 pain — CoachSidebar.tsx still dumps 8-10 flat tiles; the native 4+Mas pattern is not adopted on web.
No mention that removing `exercises` from NAV_MODULES breaks coach-nav.test.ts and the nav-title E2E specs — those must be updated in the same change.

No parallel `featurePref` hook in getVisibleNavItems — the registry only knows `entitlement`, so hiding an ENTITLED base menu (Nutricion now, Ejercicios later) per coach is not yet possible despite being a stated goal.

Naming collision unresolved: /coach/settings is already titled 'Opciones'; the new 'Opciones Coach' button needs a distinct name, and its relationship to the existing read-only /coach/settings/modules (ModulesForm) is undefined.
goals_bodycomp module mapping (body_composition vs nutrition_exchanges) is wrong/ambiguous in the section table.
Resolver omits team-pool entitlement precedence and the EVA_DISABLED_MODULES operator kill-switch present in hasExchangesModuleForClientContext.
No guided empty-states spec for on-but-no-data surfaces (NN/g pitfall) — only off/hidden is covered.

mobile app (apps/mobile) parity: it talks PostgREST directly and has its own tab registry (ejercicios tab, settings/subscription/perfil) — the consolidation and prefs resolver need a mobile counterpart or they drift.

roles.role: Mobile Engineer (iOS/Android — Expo SDK 54 / expo-router v6, apps/mobile)
roles.verdict: Conditional go. The web-side model (visible = ENTITLED AND ENABLED, preference only narrows; side tables to dodge the column-grant gotcha) is sound and the Ejercicios-into-Programas consolidation is genuinely low-risk. BUT the plan is written almost entirely for apps/web and treats the native app as an afterthought. apps/mobile is a separate, hand-rolled nav (PRIMARY_TABS + NAV_META) that talks PostgREST directly with its own RLS-bound queries and NO server-side entitlement guard layer. If the feature_prefs system ships web-only, mobile coaches/alumnos get a DIFFERENT, un-narrowed app — silent cross-surface drift that the founder's 'one consolidated experience' goal explicitly forbids. The prefs resolver and the consolidation must be specified as @eva/* shared logic from day one, or mobile becomes the thing that breaks current coaches.

topConcerns.title: feature_prefs is web-only; mobile renders its own un-narrowed nav and screens
topConcerns.detail: apps/mobile/components/coach/CoachMobileChrome.tsx hardcodes nav as PRIMARY_TABS=['home','clientes','builder','nutricion'] + a NAV_META map + an (tabs)/_layout with ~10 registered screens. It does NOT import coach-nav.ts and has no getVisibleNavItems equivalent. The plan's third AND-condition (coach preference) lives in web's getVisibleNavItems and a web resolver. If prefs ship web-only, a coach who disables Nutricion (or Ejercicios) on web still sees it in the native app, and an alumno whose coach hid a section still sees that section in the alumno mobile app. That is exactly the 'broke things current coaches rely on' + cross-surface inconsistency the founder wants to AVOID. The resolver and the section catalog MUST be packaged as @eva/* (like @eva/types/@eva/schemas/@eva/brand-kit, the established anti-drift pattern in apps/mobile/AGENTS.md) so both surfaces consume one source of truth.

topConcerns.title: Mobile has NO server-side entitlement guard — prefs+entitlement must be enforced in RLS/RPC, not just app code
topConcerns.detail: Web enforces entitlement server-side via assertModule/hasModule before rendering. Mobile (per apps/mobile/lib/coach-client-detail.ts header: 'service-role never runs in RN') reads tables directly under the coach/alumno RLS session; there is no assertModule on the native path. So for mobile, the ONLY real gate is RLS + RPC. If feature_prefs visibility is enforced only in React (web component tree), the mobile client simply won't apply it, and worse, a hidden-but-entitled section's DATA is still fully readable from the device. Decision needed: prefs are a presentation-only narrowing (acceptable IF every surface, incl. mobile, applies the SAME shared resolver) — and that must be written down, because mobile cannot lean on a server guard that doesn't exist for it.

topConcerns.title: client_feature_prefs alumno-read RLS will silently fail on mobile (clients.id != auth.uid())
topConcerns.detail: The gap-analysis already flagged the migration uses client_id = auth.uid(). On mobile this is the dominant read path: the alumno app reads its own prefs directly via PostgREST (no server shim to paper over a wrong predicate). If the policy is wrong, getStudent-style queries return zero rows and EVERY optional section renders OFF for every alumno on mobile — a total nutrition/section blackout that won't show in web QA. Must copy the real alumno->clients predicate (clients.user_id = auth.uid(), verify against bodycomp_student_self_select) AND add a mobile RLS smoke test reading prefs as an authenticated alumno.

topConcerns.title: Mobile tab registration is static; you cannot conditionally hide an expo-router screen the way web filters a registry
topConcerns.detail: expo-router registers screens declaratively in (tabs)/_layout.tsx. Removing 'ejercicios' as a top-level destination (the consolidation) and hiding entitled-but-disabled modules requires either keeping the screen registered but excluded from PRIMARY_TABS/overflow (current CoachMobileChrome filters routes by name), or using href:null/redirect. The plan's 'remove the NAV_MODULES entry' is web-only mechanics. The mobile equivalent (drop from NAV_META/overflow list, keep route alive for deep links) is a DIFFERENT change that must be specified, or the native 'Más' sheet keeps showing Ejercicios while web doesn't.

topConcerns.title: The 'Opciones hub with 3 buttons' has no mobile home; mobile already splits account into 3 destinations
topConcerns.detail: Mobile NAV_META has settings(Mi Marca), subscription(Plan), perfil(Mi cuenta) as THREE separate overflow destinations. The founder's consolidation collapses these into one Opciones hub. On web that's a settings/page refactor; on mobile it means restructuring the (tabs) screens + overflow list and routing branding/subscription/funciones as sub-screens under one tab. If only web consolidates, the two apps' IA diverge further, not less. The new 'Opciones Coach / Funciones' zone also needs a native screen, not just a web page.

topConcerns.title: Mobile reads must apply prefs at the QUERY layer, not just hide widgets, to honor hide-don't-delete + dependent widgets
topConcerns.detail: coach-client-detail.ts fetches nutrition meals, timelines, compliance, macros in one big Promise.all and the alumno dashboard renders macro rings/compliance from it. If nutrition (or a sub-section) is disabled by coach pref, mobile must gate the nav entry AND the dependent dashboard tiles (macro ring, compliance) together — the B2B2C research explicitly calls out 'disable parent while dependent widget still renders' as a break. On mobile this is manual (no shared component gating like web), so the shared resolver output must be threaded into these RN render paths deliberately.

topConcerns.title: No offline/stale-prefs consideration for the PWA/native client
topConcerns.detail: Mobile (and the PWA sw.js) can render from cached data. A coach toggling a section OFF won't propagate instantly to an offline-opened alumno app; the section reappears until refetch. Not a blocker, but the plan should state the expectation (prefs are best-effort eventual on client; the server/RLS read is the source of truth on next fetch) so it isn't treated as a bug later.

improvements.title: Package the prefs resolver + section catalog as @eva/feature-prefs (shared), consumed by web AND apps/mobile
improvements.detail: Mirror the existing @eva/module-catalog / @eva/types pattern. Put the pure FeatureSections config, the preset maps (basico/intermedio/profesional), the requiresModule:ModuleKey hooks, and a framework-agnostic resolveFeaturePrefs(entitlements, coachPrefs, clientPrefs?) -> visibleSections function in a package. Web's getVisibleNavItems and mobile's CoachMobileChrome/coach-client-detail both call it. This is the only way to guarantee the 'one consolidated experience' across surfaces and is consistent with apps/mobile/AGENTS.md 'Reuse @eva/* — same logic = parity'. Add a catalog.test.ts-style cross-check (keys match MODULE_KEYS) as already done for module-catalog.

improvements.title: Specify the mobile nav delta explicitly in the plan (NAV_META + PRIMARY_TABS + (tabs)/_layout)
improvements.detail: For each consolidation: (a) Ejercicios -> drop from NAV_META/overflow but keep the (tabs)/ejercicios screen registered (href:null or excluded from overflow list) so deep links survive; surface it as an in-page button on the builder/Programas screen. (b) Mi Marca/Suscripcion/perfil -> collapse into one Opciones overflow entry that opens a native hub screen with 3 rows. (c) Module items (cardio/movement) aren't even in mobile NAV_META today — confirm whether they should be, so prefs don't gate something that's already absent and create new asymmetry.

improvements.title: Make the alumno-read RLS predicate correct and add a native RLS smoke test
improvements.detail: Fix client_feature_prefs/coach_feature_prefs client-read policies to clients.user_id = auth.uid() (verify against an existing alumno-self policy like bodycomp_student_self_select / met_client_select). Because mobile hits PostgREST directly, add a test that authenticates as an alumno JWT and asserts it can read its coach's prefs row — web QA will not catch this.

improvements.title: Decide and document prefs = presentation-narrowing applied on EVERY client; entitlement stays the hard gate
improvements.detail: Since mobile has no service-role/assertModule path, write the rule: ENTITLEMENT is enforced by RLS/RPC (hard, data-level); PREFERENCE is a narrowing applied identically by the shared @eva resolver on web + mobile (soft, presentation). Explicitly accept that a hidden-but-entitled section's data may still be fetchable on-device (it's hidden, not access-controlled) — or, if that's unacceptable, the prefs must move into RLS/RPC, which is a much larger change. Pick one in the plan; today it's ambiguous and mobile makes the ambiguity load-bearing.

improvements.title: Gate dependent mobile widgets with the resolver output, not ad hoc
improvements.detail: In coach-client-detail.ts / alumno dashboard, derive a single visibleSections object once and use it to gate both the nav destination and the dependent tiles (macro ring, compliance, timeline). Keep fetching data (hide-don't-delete), just don't render. Add this as an explicit task so a future dev doesn't wire 'disable' to a mutation (the nutrition_meal_logs ON DELETE CASCADE data-loss trap).

improvements.title: Reuse the existing native 'More overflow' pattern for the consolidated bar
improvements.detail: Good news: mobile already solved the clutter the web bar suffers (4 primary + Más). The plan should NOT reinvent it; it should (a) keep PRIMARY_TABS at 4, (b) route the now-fewer secondary items through Más, and (c) bring web's responsive bar UP to this pattern (the research's 4+More recommendation) so the two surfaces finally match.

roles.biggestRisk: Cross-surface drift becomes a regression for CURRENT coaches: the plan ships feature_prefs + menu consolidation on apps/web while apps/mobile keeps its own static, un-narrowed nav and direct-PostgREST reads. The instant a coach (or alumno) opens the native app, they see a different menu and sections the coach thought they disabled — and a wrong alumno-read RLS predicate (clients.id=auth.uid()) silently zeroes out every optional section on mobile with no web-QA signal. The fix is non-negotiable: the resolver + section catalog must be shared @eva/* code applied identically on both surfaces, and the alumno-read policy must use clients.user_id=auth.uid() with a native authenticated-read test.
missing: No mobile/native plan at all: apps/mobile (CoachMobileChrome PRIMARY_TABS/NAV_META, (tabs)/_layout, lib/coach-client-detail.ts) is not in the consolidation or prefs design despite being a shipped coach+alumno surface.
No statement that the resolver/section catalog is shared (@eva/*) — without it web and mobile WILL diverge (violates the anti-drift rule in apps/mobile/AGENTS.md).

No enforcement story for mobile: mobile has no assertModule/service-role; the plan assumes a server guard that doesn't exist on the native path, so prefs-as-presentation-only must be explicit and the alumno-read RLS must actually work under a device JWT.
No alumno mobile read test for client_feature_prefs (the wrong client_id=auth.uid() predicate would blackout sections silently).
No expo-router mechanics for hiding/consolidating a registered tab screen (href:null/redirect vs excluding from overflow) — different from web's 'remove registry entry'.
No native screen for the new 'Opciones Coach / Funciones' zone, and no plan to collapse mobile's 3 account destinations (settings/subscription/perfil) into the hub.
No offline/PWA-cache + stale-prefs expectation (toggles won't propagate to an offline-open client until refetch).
No dependent-widget gating spec for mobile (macro ring/compliance render from the same bundle as the nutrition section).

roles.verdict: Plan is directionally sound and the architecture (entitlement vs preference separation, side-tables to dodge the column-grant gotcha, hide-never-delete) is correct and matches EVA's existing patterns. But it is NOT ready to ship as-is: there is a shared-function redefinition collision under merge_branch replay, no migration backfill/grandfathering strategy for existing coaches, an unguarded DROP TABLE, and a nav-registry change that breaks tests. Two corrections to the prior audit: (1) the client_id = auth.uid() RLS predicate is CORRECT for EVA's standalone legacy identity (clients.id = auth.uid()), not a P0 break; (2) the real P0 is the shared updated_at function. Approve to build only after the migration-safety items below are folded into F0.

topConcerns.title: Shared nutrition_set_updated_at() redefined with different body — replay collision
topConcerns.detail: 20260618180001_nutrient_targets.sql already defines public.nutrition_set_updated_at() as LANGUAGE plpgsql WITHOUT SET search_path. The feature_prefs migration (line 9-11) does CREATE OR REPLACE of the SAME function name WITH search_path=''. Both are forward-only and merge_branch replays ALL history in timestamp order: whichever runs last wins and silently mutates the function for ALL 6 nutrition tables (nutrient_targets, intake_entries, shopping_list_items, meal_comments, recipes, plus these two). A function-definition divergence across migrations is exactly the kind of non-deterministic-on-replay hazard CLAUDE.md warns about. Fix: do NOT redefine it — either reuse the existing one as-is, or rename the new one feature_prefs_set_updated_at(). The audit's cosmetic 'rename for genericity' note understated this: it is a correctness bug, not cosmetics.

topConcerns.title: No backfill / grandfathering — existing coaches silently lose nutrition surfaces
topConcerns.detail: The model is visible = ENTITLED AND ENABLED, and ENABLED comes from coach_feature_prefs. Every current coach has NO row (the table is new).
