The resolver must therefore treat 'no row' as the EXISTING behavior, not as the new default. If the resolver defaults missing prefs to a preset (e.g. 'basico' = most sections OFF), every coach already using recetas/habitos/registro-fuera-de-plan loses them on deploy with zero warning. June-2026 migration research is unanimous: a column/config default does NOT grandfather anyone; you must explicitly backfill or resolve absence to current state. The plan's F0 says nothing about this. Required: either (a) backfill a 'profesional'/all-on row for coaches with existing nutrition usage (has nutrition_plans or nutrition_meal_logs), default-OFF only for net-new coaches; or (b) make the resolver's no-row branch return all-currently-rendered-sections-ON. Pick one and write it into F0 with the usage query.

topConcerns.title: Unguarded DROP TABLE under forward-only replay
topConcerns.detail: Lines 6-7 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS coach_nutrition_prefs / client_nutrition_prefs with no emptiness guard. merge_branch re-runs the whole history; if those tables ever receive data (or a future migration writes to them before this one in a replay), the DROP is silent data loss and violates the 'jamas DROP destructivo' rule in CLAUDE.md. Confirm 0 rows via list_tables/execute_sql BEFORE shipping, and add a comment asserting they are empty scaffolding. Also grep database.types.ts and all repos for the old names before regen.

topConcerns.title: Permissive-policy multiplication on coach_feature_prefs
topConcerns.detail: coach_feature_prefs has THREE policies that all grant SELECT to authenticated: owner_all (FOR ALL includes SELECT), client_read (SELECT), team_read (SELECT). Postgres OR-stacks permissive policies → multiple_permissive_policies, the exact perf-advisor warning the DB audit drove from 575→510. This table is read on every coach AND every alumno nutrition render (hot path). Run get_advisors after apply; consider consolidating the read branches into one policy (owner OR client OR pool) like bcm_select does in a single USING.

topConcerns.title: Nav-registry edit breaks unit + E2E tests; resolver ignores team/kill-switch context
topConcerns.detail: Removing the 'exercises' entry from NAV_MODULES touches coach-nav.test.ts and the E2E specs that assert nav titles (collectNavTitles / aside nav a[title]) — must be updated in the same change, route /coach/exercises kept alive. Separately, the design-doc resolver signature resolveFeaturePrefs(coachId, clientId?) reduces entitlement to coachId, but EVA's real Pro gate (hasExchangesModuleForClientContext) resolves by resource context where the TEAM POOL wins, and applies the EVA_DISABLED_MODULES operator kill-switch. A team-pool alumno will get wrong Pro gating unless the resolver threads team/org context and reuses findPlanModuleContext + the kill-switch verbatim instead of a bare coachId lookup.

improvements.title: Make the no-row resolver branch return current behavior, and write the backfill query in F0
improvements.detail: In resolveFeaturePrefs, the absence of a coach_feature_prefs row must NOT collapse to preset 'basico'. Either backfill (INSERT ... SELECT coaches with EXISTS nutrition_plans/nutrition_meal_logs → preset profesional/all-on; everyone else default-OFF) as a separate batched idempotent step, or branch: no row → all rendered-today sections ON. This is the single change that converts 'don't break current coaches' from prose into code. Add a vitest asserting 'no prefs row ⇒ section X visible' for every section that renders today.

improvements.title: Reuse, don't redefine, the updated_at trigger function
improvements.detail: Drop lines 9-11 of feature_prefs.sql. If search_path hardening is wanted, do it as its own dedicated migration that updates the single canonical function once, with a comment that all *_set_updated_at triggers depend on it — do not fork the definition per-table.

improvements.title: Add Zod validation + safe default for preset/domain on the write path
improvements.detail: domain and preset have no CHECK (deliberate, for genericity). That pushes validation to the app: the server action must Zod-validate preset ∈ {basico,intermedio,profesional} and domain ∈ known set, and the resolver must treat null/unknown preset as the safe default so s.presets[pref.preset] never indexes undefined → silent OFF.

improvements.title: Reframe 'new Opciones hub' as a restructure of the existing /coach/settings
improvements.detail: /coach/settings already has metadata title 'Opciones' and already renders a 3-card hub in team_managed mode. This is not greenfield. Decide explicitly whether branding moves to /coach/settings/brand (so 'Mi Marca' becomes a card) and reconcile that both the brand and settings_team nav entries already route to /coach/settings context-aware. Relate the NEW Funciones zone (preference toggles) to the EXISTING /coach/settings/modules (read-only entitlement catalog) with a one-line explainer so coaches don't confuse 'what I bought' vs 'what I show'. Reuse MODULE_CATALOG copy for the Pro/upsell strings rather than re-authoring.

improvements.title: Add a feature-sections catalog test mirroring catalog.test.ts
improvements.detail: The FeatureSections config is the source of truth and must stay in sync with ModuleKey. Add the same cross-check discipline as MODULE_CATALOG_KEYS/MODULE_KEYS: a test asserting every requiresModule value is a valid ModuleKey and every section has a defined value in all 3 presets.

improvements.title: Run get_advisors (security+performance) after apply and snapshot before
improvements.detail: Per the DB workflow: snapshot _bak before, apply additive DDL, then get_advisors expecting 0 criticals — specifically watch multiple_permissive_policies on coach_feature_prefs. Regenerate database.types.ts after (apply_migration does not write the local .sql or types).

roles.biggestRisk: Silent regression for existing coaches on deploy day. The visible = ENTITLED AND ENABLED model means any coach without a coach_feature_prefs row (i.e. 100% of current coaches at launch) gets their ENABLED set entirely from defaults. If the resolver's no-row path resolves to a restrictive preset, coaches who today rely on recetas / habitos / registro-fuera-de-plan / micros will see those surfaces vanish with no error, no log, no upsell — it just looks like EVA deleted features. There is no backfill in the plan and no test pinning 'no row ⇒ current behavior'. This is invisible (nothing throws), affects every paying coach simultaneously, and directly contradicts the founder's hard constraint 'MUST NOT break things current coaches rely on'. It must be solved in F0 before any UI ships.
missing: No backfill / grandfathering migration for the ~all existing coaches who have zero prefs rows (the central 'don't break current coaches' gap).
No mention that nutrition_set_updated_at() already exists in a prior migration with a different body — collision on merge_branch replay.

No rollback / kill-switch plan: if the prefs resolver misbehaves in prod there is no flag to bypass it back to 'show everything' (consider an env or Edge Config short-circuit in the resolver during rollout, mirroring PROXY_USE_GETCLAIMS).

No data-loss guardrail codified: the 'toggle = render only, never delete' invariant is prose, not a PR-checklist rule or a test asserting a Funcion toggle writes ONLY to *_feature_prefs.sections and touches no nutrition_* row (the nutrition_meal_logs ON DELETE CASCADE foot-gun).

No dependent-widget gating: turning Nutrición OFF must also drop dependent dashboard surfaces (MacroBar / ComplianceRing consume nutrition) — research flagged the orphan-widget pitfall; plan only addresses top-level sections.
No coach-nav featurePref hook: founder goal (2) includes coach prefs over base nav functions, but getVisibleNavItems only knows entitlement, not preference — adding a parallel hook is unscoped.

No apps/mobile + apps/enterprise impact assessment: those clients hit PostgREST directly and read the same RLS; the new tables' alumno-read policies and any base-function hiding must be verified against the Expo coach/alumno apps, not just web.
No advisors/snapshot step or database.types.ts regen called out in F0 DoD.

roles.verdict: Plan is directionally sound and the ENTITLED ∧ ENABLED model is correct, but it is NOT yet test-ready or migration-safe. Two backend items are blocking for "don't break current coaches": (1) the migration ships with zero backfill, so every existing coach/alumno with no prefs row silently collapses to Básico and all currently-visible advanced nutrition surfaces disappear with no test guarding it; (2) the resolver signature in the design doc (coachId, clientId?) does NOT match the real entitlement resolver which keys off planId via findPlanModuleContext — team-pool alumnos get wrong Pro gating. Both must be fixed and covered by tests before any UI lands. Approve to build only with the corrections + the test matrix below.

topConcerns.title: No backfill = silent regression for every existing coach/alumno (grandfathering)
topConcerns.detail: The migration creates coach_feature_prefs/client_feature_prefs empty. The resolver defaults preset to 'basico' when no row exists (design §7c: s.presets[coachPrefs.preset]). Basico = ONLY core (plan/macros/adherence). So the day F2 wires the shells, every existing coach who today sees recetas/lista de compras/habitos/registro fuera de plan/micros (data already created via migrations 20260618180001-180005) LOSES those surfaces with no warning. June-2026 research (GitLab MR122467, fly.io) is explicit: a new column/row default does NOT grandfather existing rows — you must explicitly backfill. Required: a batched, idempotent backfill that derives each existing coach's preset from observed usage (has recipes/shopping/intake/notes rows or non-empty plans → 'intermedio'/'profesional'; else 'basico'). Without it this is a guaranteed prod regression the moment rendering goes conditional. There is currently NO test asserting 'existing coach keeps current surfaces'.

topConcerns.title: Resolver entitlement signature mismatch — team-pool alumnos mis-gated
topConcerns.detail: Design §7c proposes resolveNutritionPrefs(coachId, clientId?) and resolveProEntitlement(coachId). But the real, fail-closed entitlement source getNutritionProEnabledForClient (sections.queries.ts:214) takes a planId and resolves context via findPlanModuleContext → hasExchangesModuleForClientContext({clientTeamId, clientOrgId, planCoachId}) where the TEAM POOL wins over the plan coach. Reducing this to bare coachId means a pool alumno whose Pro entitlement comes from teams.enabled_modules (not the plan coach's) gets the wrong answer — either leaking Pro or hiding paid features. Resolver MUST consume the same resource context (planId or {coachId, clientTeamId, clientOrgId}) and reuse findPlanModuleContext + hasExchangesModuleForClientContext verbatim, not reimplement. This is a paywall-correctness bug, not cosmetic.

topConcerns.title: Operator kill-switch not threaded in the resolver pseudocode
topConcerns.detail: PLAN doc model line includes 'AND NOT killed (operador)' but the design §7c resolver pseudocode (entitled = s.requiresModule ? proEnabled : true) omits isModuleKilledByOperator/applyOperatorKillSwitch entirely. hasModule already applies the kill-switch; if the new resolver calls a thinner entitlement path it can re-expose a module the operator killed via EVA_DISABLED_MODULES. Test needed: with EVA_DISABLED_MODULES=nutrition_exchanges, micros_advanced/goals_bodycomp resolve OFF even for an entitled+enabled coach.

topConcerns.title: Permissive-policy stacking on coach_feature_prefs (perf advisor regression)
topConcerns.detail: coach_feature_prefs has THREE policies usable for SELECT: owner_all (FOR ALL → includes SELECT), client_read (FOR SELECT), team_read (FOR SELECT). Postgres evaluates them as multiple_permissive_policies for the authenticated/SELECT pair — exactly the advisor warning the DB audit fought down 575→510. Same shape on client_feature_prefs (coach_all FOR ALL + client_read + team_all FOR ALL → multiple permissive for SELECT and for ALL). Run get_advisors after apply; expect new perf warnings. Consider splitting owner_all into explicit INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE + a single consolidated SELECT, or accept and document. Not a correctness bug but it violates the project's own advisor-clean bar.

topConcerns.title: Nav consolidation (Ejercicios→Programas) will break existing nav tests with no test-update plan
topConcerns.detail: NAV_MODULES is pure and unit-tested (coach-nav.test.ts) and E2E specs assert nav titles via aside nav a[title]. Removing the exercises entry breaks coach-nav unit tests and any E2E asserting title 'Ejercicios'. The plan mentions the move but not the test churn. Also: /coach/exercises route AND the client /c/[coach_slug]/exercises must stay reachable (deep links/bookmarks) — only the nav surfacing moves. And the mobile app (apps/mobile) registers an 'ejercicios' tab + overflow independently; consolidating web without touching mobile creates cross-surface drift. QA needs: updated coach-nav.test.ts, a test asserting /coach/exercises still 200s, and an E2E that the new colored 'Lista de ejercicios' button on the Programas page navigates correctly.

improvements.title: Add an explicit backfill migration + a regression test as F0 acceptance criteria
improvements.detail: Ship the backfill in the SAME change as feature_prefs (idempotent, batched, derives preset from usage: query for any rows in nutrition_recipes/shopping_list_items/intake_entries/private notes/nutrient_targets per coach's clients → intermedio/profesional, else basico). Add a vitest that snapshots resolveNutritionPrefs for a coach WITH advanced data and asserts those sections stay true after migration. This is the single most important 'don't break current coaches' guard and it currently does not exist.

improvements.title: Verified: clients.id = auth.uid() in the RLS policies is CORRECT for EVA
improvements.detail: One upstream auditor flagged coach_feature_prefs_client_read / client_feature_prefs_client_read as broken (claimed alumno maps via clients.user_id). I checked migration 20260613120000_bodycomp_student_self_select.sql — the codebase comment explicitly states 'identidad legacy clients.id = auth.uid(), mismo criterio que dashboard/check-in y la vista de movimiento del alumno'. So the migration's c.id = (SELECT auth.uid()) and client_id = (SELECT auth.uid()) predicates match EVA's canonical alumno-self pattern. Do NOT 'fix' them to user_id — that would actually break alumno reads. Add an RLS isolation test anyway (alumno A cannot read coach B's prefs; alumno reads own coach's prefs).

improvements.title: Build the resolver around planId/resource-context from day one
improvements.detail: Type the resolver as resolveNutritionPrefs(ctx: {planId} | {coachId, clientTeamId?, clientOrgId?}, clientId?) and internally call findPlanModuleContext + hasExchangesModuleForClientContext (exchanges.repository.ts + nutrition-exchanges.service.ts). Reuse, don't reimplement. Add gating tests mirroring nutrition-exchanges.service.gating.test.ts: pool-Pro-on/plan-coach-Pro-off, plan-coach-Pro-on/pool-off, standalone.

improvements.title: Add the preset/domain validation the migration deliberately dropped
improvements.detail: Migration has no CHECK on preset or domain (intentional for genericity). Compensate in code: resolver treats null/unknown preset as 'basico' (never index undefined into s.presets), and the write server action Zod-validates preset ∈ {basico,intermedio,profesional} and domain ∈ allowed set. Add a test: a row with garbage preset resolves to basico (core-only), not all-OFF.

improvements.title: Rename nutrition_set_updated_at → generic and grep for an existing set_updated_at first
improvements.detail: The trigger fn is public.nutrition_set_updated_at() but the tables are generic *_feature_prefs. Contradicts the genericity thesis and confuses the next dev. Reuse an existing generic set_updated_at if one exists; else name it feature_prefs_set_updated_at. Cosmetic but cheap.

improvements.title: Guard the DROP TABLE and confirm 0 rows + no type-gen references before shipping
improvements.detail: Lines 6-7 unconditionally DROP coach_nutrition_prefs/client_nutrition_prefs. Because merge_branch replays ALL history forward-only, if those ever held data it's silently destroyed. Confirm via list_tables they are empty, confirm database.types.ts has no consumers of the old names, regen types after apply. Add a comment documenting they were empty scaffolding.

improvements.title: Adopt the native '4 + Más' pattern for the web responsive bottom bar
improvements.detail: The biggest real clutter is the web responsive bottom bar (CoachSidebar.tsx) dumping 8-10 items into one horizontal scroll — strictly worse than apps/mobile which already does 4 PRIMARY_TABS + Más. The menu consolidation goal isn't met by just removing Ejercicios; QA should require the web mobile bar to mirror the native overflow pattern, with an E2E asserting primary tabs render and secondary items live behind 'Más'.

improvements.title: Test the dependent-widget cascade, not just the nav tab
improvements.detail: Research (NN/g empty states) + EVA reality: MacroBar/ComplianceRing on the dashboard consume nutrition. If a coach disables a nutrition section, dependent dashboard widgets must hide together or you get on-but-empty/blank surfaces. Add tests that toggling a section OFF hides BOTH the nutrition surface AND its dependent dashboard tiles, and that on-but-empty (e.g. no plan assigned) shows a guided empty state, never a blank.

roles.biggestRisk: Silent grandfathering failure. The migration plus the basico-default resolver means the instant rendering becomes conditional (F2), every existing coach who currently uses recetas / lista de compras / hábitos / registro fuera de plan / micros — surfaces whose DATA already exists from the 20260618180001-180005 migrations — loses them with no backfill, no migration warning, and no test catching it. It will read in prod as 'EVA deleted my features'. The fix (usage-derived backfill + a regression test asserting existing coaches keep their surfaces) is mandatory and currently entirely absent from the plan.
missing: No backfill / grandfathering migration for existing coaches and alumnos (the #1 'don't break current coaches' gap).

No test matrix defined: missing RLS isolation tests (alumno cross-tenant read), resolver gating tests (pool vs plan-coach Pro), kill-switch test, AND-compose/core-always-ON test, and a 'existing coach keeps surfaces' regression test.

No data-loss invariant test proving a section toggle writes ONLY to *_feature_prefs.sections jsonb and never deletes/nulls a nutrition_* row (the ON DELETE CASCADE gotcha is described in prose but not enforced by a test).

apps/mobile is unaddressed: mobile registers ejercicios/nutricion/settings tabs independently; consolidation + prefs visibility on web without a mobile parity plan creates cross-surface drift (coach sees a section on web, not on phone, or vice versa).

No coach_addons revocation/downgrade path: when a coach's Pro lapses (admin_grant revoke / dunning), the plan says entitlement re-checks server-side per render (good) but there's no test that a stale client cache or an enabled toggle cannot keep a Pro section visible after entitlement loss.
No get_advisors run planned post-migration despite the new permissive-policy stacking — the project's own bar is 0 critical advisors.

Naming collision unresolved: the page is already titled 'Opciones' (settings_team nav + metadata) and the plan adds an 'Opciones Coach' button — two things named Opciones. Also Funciones (preference) vs the existing read-only /coach/settings/modules (entitlement) need a one-line in-UI distinction or coaches will confuse 'what I bought' with 'what I show'.
No QA acceptance criteria / DoD checklist in the PLAN doc itself — for an SDD-governed repo this feature needs specs/[feature]/TASKS.md with a testable DoD before code.

roles.verdict: Conditional GO. The architecture is sound and the side-table approach correctly dodges the coaches/clients column-grant gotcha. But the draft migration ships with two real authorization defects (a privilege-escalation surface and an over-broad client write grant) and the entire "entitlement gate" lives only in app code with zero DB enforcement. Fix the RLS write-paths and add a server-side entitlement re-check at every render point before this touches prod. Do NOT let the preference layer become a path to surface paid/Pro features without entitlement.

topConcerns.title: client_feature_prefs grants INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE to authenticated with a client-self FOR ALL... actually the alumno cannot write, but the coach WITH CHECK is the only integrity guard and the table is writable by authenticated broadly
topConcerns.detail: Line 48 GRANTs INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on client_feature_prefs to authenticated; the row is protected ONLY by the coach_all policy (coach owns the client) and team_all (pool). There is no policy letting a non-coach non-pool authenticated user write — good — BUT verify with a negative test that a logged-in ALUMNO (clients.id = auth.uid()) cannot INSERT/UPDATE their own client_feature_prefs row: client_feature_prefs_client_read is SELECT-only, so an alumno write should be denied by default-deny. This MUST be proven with a tx-rollback test as authenticated impersonating the alumno, not assumed. If any policy accidentally lets the alumno write, the alumno could re-enable a section the coach disabled. EVA has shipped exactly this class of RLS leak before (check_ins, workout_sessions fixes).

topConcerns.title: The 'preference only narrows, never widens entitlement' invariant has ZERO database enforcement — it is app-resolver-only
topConcerns.detail: The whole money-safety boundary (a coach toggle/override can never surface nutrition_exchanges/body_composition Pro features to a client without the paid module) lives entirely in resolveFeaturePrefs() in app code. RLS lets a coach write ANY sections jsonb for their own client, including {nutrition_exchanges: true}. If the resolver forgets to AND with hasExchangesModuleForClientContext()/hasModule() at even one render site (coach ficha NutritionTabB5 OR alumno NutritionShell OR a dashboard widget like MacroBar/ComplianceRing that reads nutrition directly), a paid feature leaks for free. The model must be: server resolver ALWAYS recomputes visible = ENTITLED(hasModule, server) AND NOT killed(EVA_DISABLED_MODULES) AND ENABLED(prefs); the jsonb is untrusted input that can only subtract. Centralize this in ONE resolver consumed everywhere; forbid any component reading prefs.sections directly.

topConcerns.title: Resolver entitlement input is under-specified for team/pool and ignores the operator kill-switch
topConcerns.detail: resolveFeaturePrefs(coachId, clientId?, domain) reduces entitlement to the plan coach, but EVA's real entitlement resolves by resource context where the TEAM POOL wins (hasExchangesModuleForClientContext / findPlanModuleContext read clientTeamId then plan coach) and hasModule applies isModuleKilledByOperator. If the resolver gates Pro by bare coachId, a team-pool alumno gets WRONG Pro gating (either leak or false-deny), and a killed module still renders. Reuse findPlanModuleContext + hasExchangesModuleForClientContext + the kill-switch verbatim; do not reimplement entitlement.

topConcerns.title: client_feature_prefs has overlapping permissive FOR ALL policies that multiply and a team_all that may over-grant
topConcerns.detail: client_feature_prefs has coach_all (FOR ALL), client_read (SELECT), and team_all (FOR ALL) — three permissive policies stack (the same multiple_permissive_policies perf class your DB audit fought 575->510). More importantly team_all is FOR ALL over current_user_pool_client_ids(): any active team_member coach can write overrides for any client in the shared pool. That may be intended (pool = flat full-access) but it means co-coaches can silently change what an alumno sees with no per-coach audit. Confirm this is desired for team mode and run get_advisors after apply.

topConcerns.title: Unconditional DROP TABLE of coach_nutrition_prefs/client_nutrition_prefs in a forward-only, history-replaying migration
topConcerns.detail: Lines 6-7 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS with no empty-guard. CLAUDE.md mandates additive/idempotent because merge_branch replays ALL history and resets prod. If those tables ever held data (or a future re-seed populates them before this replays), this silently destroys data. Confirm 0 rows via list_tables/execute_sql before shipping, and add a defensive comment. Also grep that database.types.ts and no other migration references the old names; regen types after.

improvements.title: Add explicit RLS negative tests as authenticated (not service_role) before merge
improvements.detail: Per CLAUDE.md RLS protocol: tx-rollback with SET LOCAL ROLE authenticated + SET request.jwt.claims. Prove: (a) alumno (clients.id=auth.uid()) CANNOT write their own client_feature_prefs row; (b) coach A cannot read/write coach B's coach_feature_prefs; (c) coach A cannot write client_feature_prefs for a client they do not own; (d) a pool co-coach CAN (if intended) and a non-pool coach CANNOT; (e) alumno can SELECT their own prefs. Add these to tests/separation/ alongside the existing module-grants.sql drift check.

improvements.title: Zod-validate domain and preset on the write server action; treat null/unknown preset as 'basico'
improvements.detail: The migration intentionally has no CHECK on domain/preset (genericity). That moves validation entirely to the app: the server action MUST Zod-enforce domain in a known set and preset in ('basico','intermedio','profesional'). The resolver MUST default null/unknown preset to 'basico' (safest) so a garbage preset indexed as presets[undefined] does not silently flip sections OFF (or worse, leave an advanced section in an undefined state).

improvements.title: Make the entitlement re-check a single choke point and gate dependent widgets together
improvements.detail: Build resolveFeaturePrefs as the ONLY way any surface learns visibility, returning a resolved boolean per section (already ANDed with hasModule + kill-switch). The research is explicit: disabling nutrition must also drop dependent dashboard widgets (MacroBar, ComplianceRing consume nutrition). Gate them off the SAME resolver result, not independently, or you get inconsistent UI / a leaked Pro surface via a widget that bypassed the resolver.

improvements.title: Rename the trigger function and confirm it is generic
improvements.detail: public.nutrition_set_updated_at() on generic *_feature_prefs tables contradicts the genericity thesis and may collide. grep for an existing set_updated_at; reuse it or rename to feature_prefs_set_updated_at with SET search_path = '' (already correct).

improvements.title: Backfill existing coaches by observed usage, do not rely on the new default
improvements.detail: Research P0: a new column/row default does NOT grandfather existing rows. With no prefs row, the resolver must default to today's behavior (entitled sections visible), NOT to the new 'advanced OFF' default — otherwise every current coach who relies on, say, registro-fuera-de-plan silently loses it. Either (a) resolver treats 'no row' as 'show everything the coach is entitled to' (legacy-compatible), or (b) backfill an explicit preset='profesional' row for coaches with existing nutrition logs/plans. Do not let absence map to the restrictive new default.

roles.biggestRisk: A paid-feature leak through the preference layer: because the ENTITLED gate exists only in app code and the RLS lets a coach write any sections jsonb (including Pro keys) for their own client, a single missed AND-with-hasModule at any one render site — coach ficha, alumno shell, or a nutrition-consuming dashboard widget — surfaces nutrition_exchanges/body_composition (the $9.990/mo modules) for free, with no DB backstop to stop it. Preference must be untrusted, subtract-only input recomputed against server-side entitlement at one centralized choke point.
missing: No DB-level enforcement that preferences only narrow entitlement — the entire money-safety boundary is app-resolver trust; needs a single audited choke point + tests, since RLS cannot express 'jsonb may only subtract'.

No negative RLS test plan in the migration/plan despite CLAUDE.md mandating authenticated-impersonation tx-rollback tests; EVA has shipped this exact RLS-leak class before (check_ins, workout_sessions, IDOR streak).

Backfill/grandfathering for existing coaches is unaddressed — research flags that absence-of-row must resolve to legacy behavior, not the new restrictive default, or current coaches lose features they rely on.

get_advisors (security + performance) not scheduled after apply; three stacked permissive policies on client_feature_prefs reintroduce the multiple_permissive_policies class the prior DB audit reduced.

Audit trail: per-client override writes (and pool co-coach writes) have no audit logging — a coach/co-coach can silently change what an alumno sees; consider whether this needs an org_audit_logs-style entry for team mode.

No handling for the client_identity_split case (one account, future multiple client rows): client_feature_prefs keys on clients.id and the alumno-read policy uses clients.id = auth.uid(); confirm this still holds if a person ever has standalone + enterprise client rows (today 1:1:1, but the split layer exists).

Nav-visibility (move Ejercicios into Programas, hide entitled base menus via prefs) needs the same server-side gate: getVisibleNavItems currently only knows entitlement, not preference — a client-side-only nav hide is not a security control, the underlying route /coach/exercises must stay protected and the move must keep the assertModule-equivalent server guards intact.

roles.verdict: APPROVE WITH CHANGES. The plan is well-conceived and correctly anchored in EVA's real code: the ENTITLED AND NOT killed AND ENABLED model, side-table storage (avoids the column-grant gotcha), preference-only-narrows, hide-never-delete (CASCADE-safe), and the generic by-domain schema are all right. The migration's RLS predicate (clients.id = auth.uid()) is CORRECT for EVA (verified against baseline client_read_self and workout_plans_client_read) — contrary to one auditor's worry, EVA's alumno IS the clients row, so reads will not silently return zero. But the plan ships a real product change to LIVE coaches with NO explicit migration/backfill story for existing coaches, conflates two scopes the founder bundled (menu consolidation vs feature-visibility) into one plan with shared risk, and underspecifies the consolidation that touches the unit-tested nav registry. Fix those before build.

topConcerns.title: No backfill/grandfather story for the ~existing coach base — silent regression of nutrition surfaces
topConcerns.detail: The #1 'don't break current coaches' risk and the plan does not address it. Resolver reads coachPrefs.preset ?? defaults. A coach with NO row in coach_feature_prefs resolves to default preset = 'basico', which flips every optional nutrition section (micros base, plate method, recipes, shopping list, habits, off-plan log, notes) from currently-VISIBLE to OFF on their next render — for coaches AND their alumnos, with zero opt-in. The June-2026 research is emphatic and multi-source: a column/code default does NOT grandfather existing rows; you must explicitly backfill, and derive the default from OBSERVED USAGE (has nutrition_meal_logs / non-empty plans / micros in use -> seed 'profesional' or 'intermedio'; truly empty -> 'basico'). The plan says 'Default Básico para coaches existentes (no retroactivo a alumnos)' which is exactly the harmful path: it silently strips features a working coach relies on today. REQUIRED: F0 ships an idempotent, batched backfill that seeds each existing coach a preset/sections row inferred from current usage, so today's experience is preserved. Without it, this is a launch-day support fire and erodes trust mid-overhaul.

topConcerns.title: Two distinct founder goals (menu consolidation + feature visibility) are fused into one plan/branch with shared blast radius
topConcerns.detail: The founder's GOAL has 4 parts: consolidate menu clutter, feature enable/disable prefs, the Opciones hub, and the concrete Ejercicios->Programas move. The PLAN doc only covers the feature-visibility/Opciones-Coach half; the design doc is nutrition-only and never mentions Ejercicios->Programas, the mobile bottom-bar clutter (the audit's WORST offender: 8-10 flat horizontal-scroll tiles, no overflow), or nav consolidation at all. These are different risk profiles: nav consolidation is mostly cosmetic/low-risk and ships value immediately; the prefs engine is backend-heavy and money-adjacent (touches entitlement resolution). Bundling them means the cheap, high-confidence win (declutter) is held hostage to the slower, riskier prefs work, and one shared branch raises the chance of a regression touching coaches who rely on current nav. As PM I want these SEQUENCED: Phase A = pure consolidation (drop exercises from NAV_MODULES, add 'Lista de ejercicios' button, web mobile adopts the native '4 primary + Más' pattern that apps/mobile already uses) — independently shippable, no DB. Phase B = prefs engine. Don't gate the declutter on the prefs migration.

topConcerns.title: Nav consolidation is under-specified vs a typed, unit-tested, E2E-asserted registry
topConcerns.detail: NAV_MODULES in coach-nav.ts is the single source of truth, pure, with coach-nav.test.ts and E2E specs asserting nav titles (collectNavTitles / aside nav a[title]). 'Move Ejercicios into Programas' is not just adding a button: it requires removing the exercises entry from NAV_MODULES AND updating those tests AND keeping /coach/exercises route alive (deep links, bookmarks, the client app /c/[coach_slug]/exercises). The mobile app (apps/mobile) ALSO registers ejercicios as a tab — cross-surface drift if web changes and mobile doesn't. Also unresolved: should base nav items themselves be coach-toggleable (founder goal 2 says prefs over base functions too)? If yes, getVisibleNavItems needs a new featurePref AND-condition parallel to the existing entitlement hook — a real addition neither doc mentions. Spec this explicitly or it ships half-done.

topConcerns.title: Opciones hub naming clash and unclear relationship to the existing read-only Módulos page
topConcerns.detail: /coach/settings is ALREADY titled 'Opciones' (metadata) and is ALREADY a 3-card hub in team mode (Módulos/Áreas/Mi Equipo) — the founder's 'new hub' is a restructure, not greenfield, and the plan describes it as if new. Worse, the founder's 'Opciones Coach' button collides by name with the page already called 'Opciones'. And there are now TWO module surfaces a coach must mentally separate: /coach/settings/modules (ENTITLEMENT, read-only, 'what you bought') vs the new Funciones zone (PREFERENCE, toggles, 'what you show'). Coaches WILL confuse them. Plan must (a) pick distinct names, (b) reconcile that nav already routes both brand and settings_team to /coach/settings, (c) state explicitly in-UI that Módulos=billing/read-only and Funciones=free toggles, ideally adjacent with a one-line explainer, reusing MODULE_CATALOG copy for Pro badges instead of re-authoring.

topConcerns.title: Migration cleanup details: DROP guard, generic naming, and permissive-policy stacking
topConcerns.detail: Three real but contained backend nits. (1) The unconditional DROP TABLE IF EXISTS coach_nutrition_prefs/client_nutrition_prefs has no empty-guard; since merge_branch replays ALL history forward-only, confirm via list_tables they are 0 rows before shipping (plan asserts empty — verify, don't assume). (2) The updated_at trigger fn is named nutrition_set_updated_at on GENERIC *_feature_prefs tables — contradicts the genericity thesis; rename to feature_prefs_set_updated_at or reuse an existing generic set_updated_at (grep first). (3) coach_feature_prefs has a FOR ALL owner policy PLUS two FOR SELECT policies (client_read, team_read) — FOR ALL already includes SELECT, so three permissive SELECT policies stack (the exact multiple_permissive_policies perf issue the DB audit fought from 575->510). Run get_advisors after apply and consider consolidating.

topConcerns.title: Resolver signature omits team/pool entitlement context and the operator kill-switch
topConcerns.detail: The design's resolveNutritionPrefs(coachId, clientId?) reduces entitlement to resolveProEntitlement(coachId). But real EVA entitlement (hasExchangesModuleForClientContext) resolves by RESOURCE context — team pool wins via clientTeamId, then plan coach — and hasModule applies isModuleKilledByOperator (EVA_DISABLED_MODULES). A team-pool alumno will get wrong Pro gating if the resolver only knows coachId. The PLAN model line does mention 'NOT killed' but the design pseudocode threads neither teamId/orgId nor the kill-switch. Reuse findPlanModuleContext + hasExchangesModuleForClientContext verbatim; don't reimplement a coachId-only entitlement path.

improvements.title: Add an explicit F0 usage-based backfill (and make it the gate for F2 wiring)
improvements.detail: Before any shell reads the resolver, ship an idempotent batched migration that seeds every existing coach_feature_prefs row from observed usage: coaches with nutrition_meal_logs / assigned nutrition plans / micros in active use -> 'profesional' or 'intermedio' (preserve what they see today); empty -> 'basico'. New coaches get 'basico'. This is the literal GitLab user_preferences precedent in the research. Without this, F2 wiring is the regression. Add a resolver test: 'coach with existing nutrition usage and no explicit toggle still sees their current sections.'

improvements.title: Split the work: ship pure menu consolidation FIRST, independent of the prefs DB
improvements.detail: Sequence Phase A (no migration): remove exercises from NAV_MODULES + 'Lista de ejercicios' outline/secondary button on the Programas list header (LibraryHeader already has a right-aligned action slot), keep /coach/exercises route alive, update coach-nav.test.ts + nav-title E2E, and fix the WORST clutter spot by making the web mobile bottom bar adopt apps/mobile's existing PRIMARY_TABS (4) + 'Más' overflow pattern instead of a flat 8-10 horizontal scroll. This ships founder value (declutter) in days with low risk and no DB. Then Phase B = prefs engine on its own branch.

improvements.title: Treat null/unknown preset as a safe default and Zod-validate writes
improvements.detail: The migration dropped the preset CHECK constraint for genericity (fine), but then the resolver MUST coerce null/unknown preset -> 'basico' (s.presets[undefined] is undefined -> section silently OFF), and the write server action MUST Zod-validate preset in {basico,intermedio,profesional} + domain against a known set. Make this explicit in F0; it's cheap and prevents garbage-preset render bugs. Mirror the @eva/module-catalog catalog.test.ts discipline with a feature-sections cross-check test.

improvements.title: Rename Opciones-Coach and co-locate Funciones with the read-only Módulos catalog
improvements.detail: Pick a name that doesn't collide with the page already titled 'Opciones'. Put the new Funciones (preference toggles) zone visually adjacent to the existing /coach/settings/modules entitlement catalog with a one-line explainer ('Módulos = lo que compraste; Funciones = lo que muestras'). Reuse MODULE_CATALOG copy/badges for the Pro-locked rows so the upsell strings stay single-sourced. Reframe the doc: this is a restructure of /coach/settings + /coach/subscription, not a greenfield hub.

improvements.title: Thread resource context + kill-switch through the resolver; reuse existing entitlement code
improvements.detail: Change resolveFeaturePrefs to take the same resource context the existing nutrition code uses (planId or {coachId, clientTeamId, clientOrgId}) and call hasExchangesModuleForClientContext + applyOperatorKillSwitch rather than a bare coachId Pro check. This guarantees team-pool alumnos and the EVA_DISABLED_MODULES kill-switch behave identically to today, and keeps one entitlement source of truth.

improvements.title: Gate dependent dashboard widgets together with their section, with guided empty states
improvements.detail: The B2B2C research found the top end-user break is a parent toggled off while a dependent widget still renders (MacroBar/ComplianceRing consume nutrition). When nutrition or a sub-section is OFF, drop its nav entry AND its dependent dashboard tiles together; for on-but-empty surfaces (plan assigned, no logs yet) show a guided empty state ('registra una comida para ver tus macros'), never a blank. Add this as an explicit acceptance criterion in F2.

roles.biggestRisk: Silent regression of the existing coach base on launch day. The plan ships a real visibility change to LIVE coaches and their alumnos but has no backfill: any coach without a prefs row defaults to 'basico', which turns OFF nutrition sections they actively use today (recipes, plate method, micros base, habits, off-plan logging) for both their app and their alumnos' app, with zero opt-in or warning. This directly violates the founder's hard constraint 'MUST NOT break things current coaches rely on,' and it lands mid-nutrition-overhaul when coaches are already adjusting — turning a feature meant to reduce overwhelm into the thing that quietly removes their tools. The fix is non-optional and cheap: a usage-derived backfill in F0 before any shell reads the resolver.
missing: No backfill/grandfather migration for existing coaches — the central 'don't break current coaches' gap (research is explicit: defaults do NOT apply retroactively, must backfill from observed usage)

Menu consolidation scope (Ejercicios->Programas, mobile bottom-bar overflow) is entirely absent from both PLAN and design docs despite being half the founder's stated goal — design doc is nutrition-only
No mobile/responsive nav story: the audit's WORST clutter (8-10 flat horizontal-scroll tiles on web mobile) is never addressed; apps/mobile already has the '4 + Más' pattern to copy
No cross-surface sync plan for apps/mobile — it registers its own ejercicios tab and nutrition screens; web nav/prefs changes drift from native unless coordinated
No rollback / kill-switch for the prefs feature itself — if the resolver misbehaves in prod, there's no documented flag to bypass it and fall back to current (show-all-entitled) behavior
No analytics/observability on preset adoption or section usage — can't tell if the declutter is working or if coaches are confused, and can't safely tune future defaults
Whether base nav items are coach-toggleable (founder goal 2 explicitly includes base functions) — getVisibleNavItems has an entitlement hook but no preference hook; unspecified

No QA/E2E plan for the entitlement-AND-preference matrix (entitled+on, entitled+off, unentitled+on=locked-upsell, unentitled+off, team-pool, per-client override) — the security-critical 'preference never widens' invariant needs an explicit test
No copy/i18n note: new Funciones UI strings must be es-latam neutral and Pro-badge strings should reuse MODULE_CATALOG, not be re-authored (drift risk)

roles.verdict: Strong, research-grounded plan with the right mental model (ENTITLED AND ENABLED, preference only narrows) and the right data shape (side-table JSONB prefs, relational entitlements). APPROVE the direction. But it is NOT ready to build as written: from a UX lens it solves the *nutrition feature-visibility* problem well while UNDER-delivering on the founder's actual #1 goal — consolidating menu clutter, which is worst on the web responsive bottom bar — and it ships an RLS bug that would silently break the alumno view (every optional section renders OFF for every student). Fix the alumno-read predicate, add the nav consolidation as a real workstream (not a side note), and add the empty-state / "don't break current coaches" guardrails before building.

topConcerns.title: Alumno-read RLS predicate is wrong → student app silently renders everything OFF
topConcerns.detail: The migration's client-read policies assume clients.id = auth.uid() (coach_feature_prefs_client_read line 33-34: c.id = auth.uid(); client_feature_prefs_client_read line 61: client_id = auth.uid()). In EVA a clients row is NOT keyed by the auth user id — student-self policies elsewhere (e.g. bodycomp_student_self_select) join on clients.user_id = auth.uid(). With the wrong predicate the alumno reads ZERO pref rows, so resolveNutritionPrefs falls back to defaults/OFF for every optional section, for every student, regardless of what the coach toggled. This is invisible in coach QA (coach owner policy works) and only breaks on the student device. UX impact: the headline promise — 'the student's screen looks different per coach config' — silently fails. Copy the real alumno→clients predicate verbatim before applying.

topConcerns.title: The founder's #1 goal — menu consolidation — is barely in the plan
topConcerns.detail: The founder explicitly framed this as NOT just a feature: (1) consolidate menu clutter, worst in responsive/mobile; (4) move Ejercicios into Programas as a colored 'Lista de ejercicios' button. The design doc is ~95% nutrition-visibility; the nav consolidation appears only as a PLAN line item ('confirmar ubicación') with no IA, no responsive spec, no Ejercicios→Programas task. The audit shows the real pain: web responsive bottom bar = 8-10 flat horizontally-scrolling tiles with ~half off-screen, NO overflow/'More' — strictly worse than the native app which already does 4 primary + Más. Research (2026) is unanimous: cap primary tabs at 3-5 + a 'More' overflow; never bury high-frequency items; same IA laddered sidebar→rail→bottombar. The plan must add an explicit nav workstream: (a) Opciones hub collapses brand+billing+coach-options (2-3 menus → 1); (b) drop 'exercises' from NAV_MODULES + add the in-page button (Pattern 1 contextual entry, validated by research); (c) give web mobile the 4-primary+Más structure CoachMobileChrome already uses.

topConcerns.title: Nav registry has no 'preference' hook — disabling an entitled base menu is unsupported today
topConcerns.detail: Founder goal (2) is coach control over base functions INCLUDING nav (e.g. a training-only coach hiding Nutrición). getVisibleNavItems in coach-nav.ts only knows contexts + entitlement; there is no featurePref dimension. The feature_prefs system as designed gates WITHIN the nutrition screen, but does NOT let a coach remove the Nutrición tab itself. If the founder wants nav-level enable/disable (and the clutter goal implies yes), the plan needs a featurePref hook in the nav registry parallel to the existing entitlement hook — currently unspecified. Decide explicitly: does Funciones toggle in-screen sections only, or also top-level nav entries? They are different scopes and the plan conflates them.

topConcerns.title: 'Don't break current coaches': no migration/grandfathering strategy for existing coaches' default
topConcerns.detail: Research is emphatic: an absent prefs row must resolve to CURRENT behavior, not the new default — a column/preset default does NOT retroactively apply, and treating 'no row' as 'Básico/OFF' silently strips features existing coaches already use. Today nutrition advanced surfaces (micros base via getPlanDayMicros, plate, etc.) are visible to everyone with a plan. The moment the resolver ships with default=Básico, every existing coach who currently shows those loses them with no warning. Plan says 'default Básico para coaches existentes (no retroactivo a alumnos)' but that IS retroactive to what the coach sees. Need: derive each existing coach's starting preset/toggles from observed usage (has plans/logs with those surfaces → seed Intermedio/ON), or one-time announce + opt-in, NOT a blanket Básico backfill.

topConcerns.title: Two 'Opciones' / naming collision + confusion with read-only Módulos catalog
topConcerns.detail: /coach/settings is ALREADY titled 'Opciones' (metadata) and the nav key settings_team already labels it 'Opciones'. The plan's new button is also 'Opciones Coach' — a clash. Separately, /coach/settings/modules (ModulesForm, read-only entitlement catalog) sits right next to the new 'Funciones' toggle zone; coaches will not distinguish 'Módulos' (what you bought, read-only) from 'Funciones' (what you show, toggles). Research pitfall: 'unpredictable destinations' + 'cute labels that don't match mental model'. Pick distinct, plain names and place Módulos + Funciones as two clearly-labeled zones in one page with a one-line explainer of the difference.

topConcerns.title: Pro-locked rows: upsell pattern is right but must avoid the 'dead toggle' anti-pattern
topConcerns.detail: Plan shows Pro rows OFF with 🔒 (good — research says show-don't-hide, Beehiiv-style value page beats dead modal). But the mock renders them as a disabled toggle with a lock, which reads as 'broken switch'. NN/g + the B2B2C research: un-entitled = upsell affordance (clickable, leads somewhere), entitled-but-off = plain toggle. Make the locked rows visually a DIFFERENT control (a 'Desbloquear con Pro' chip/CTA, not a greyed switch) so the coach learns what they gain, and route the click to the existing /coach/subscription#addons surface rather than a new upsell screen.

improvements.title: Make Ejercicios→Programas the worked example of a reusable consolidation pattern
improvements.detail: Audit confirms /coach/exercises has ZERO inbound in-app links (only the sidebar) and LibraryHeader.tsx already has a right-aligned action slot — a secondary/outline 'Lista de ejercicios' button drops in beside 'Nueva plantilla'. Keep the /coach/exercises route alive (deep links, the client /c/[slug]/exercises). Change is: remove the exercises entry from NAV_MODULES + add the button + update coach-nav.test.ts and the nav-title E2E specs (collectNavTitles asserts titles — they WILL fail). Document this as the template for the other orphans the audit found: /coach/foods (already duplicated in Nutrición hub), dead redirects /coach/templates and /coach/recipes. One consolidation pattern, several applications.

improvements.title: Spec the responsive nav explicitly — adopt the native '4 + Más' on web mobile
improvements.detail: The single highest-impact declutter is fixing CoachSidebar's mobile bottom bar (lines 188-306): replace the flat horizontal-scroll of 8-10 tiles with ~4 primary + a 'Más' overflow sheet, mirroring PRIMARY_TABS in CoachMobileChrome.tsx so web and native finally agree. After consolidation, primary becomes roughly Inicio · Alumnos · Planes · Nutri · Más, with Ejercicios folded into Planes and Marca/Plan/Soporte/account behind Más/Opciones. splitNavItems already gives you a core/modules split to drive grouping. Add this as a named phase with a responsive ladder spec (sidebar → rail → bottombar+Más).

improvements.title: Add a generic 'feature is OFF / empty' rendering contract to the spec
improvements.detail: B2B2C research + NN/g: when a section is toggled OFF, REMOVE its nav entry/tile AND any dependent dashboard widget — never leave a dead/empty surface. EVA-specific: MacroBar and ComplianceRing consume nutrition; if a coach disables nutrition sections, those widgets on the dashboard/client-detail must gate together, or you get an inconsistent UI showing a macro ring for a hidden module. And on-but-empty surfaces (plan assigned but no logs) get a guided empty state with the action in it, not a blank. Make 'gate dependent widgets together + guided empty states' a PR-review checklist item, not prose.

improvements.title: Generalize the Opciones hub from the EXISTING team-mode 3-card layout
improvements.detail: Don't build greenfield: settings/page.tsx team_managed branch (lines 26-81) is ALREADY a 3-card hub (Módulos / Áreas / Mi Equipo) — the exact visual pattern the founder wants. Generalize that to standalone: Mi Marca (split BrandSettingsForm out to /coach/settings/brand), Suscripción (/coach/subscription), Opciones Coach (new, hosts Funciones). This reuses an existing tested layout, matches the research 'one settings hub, logical tabbed categories' pattern, and removes brand+billing as separate top-level nav entries — directly shrinking the bar.

improvements.title: Fix backend hygiene in the migration before apply
improvements.detail: (1) Rename nutrition_set_updated_at → feature_prefs_set_updated_at (or reuse an existing generic set_updated_at) — the nutrition-named trigger on a generic *_feature_prefs table contradicts the genericity thesis. (2) coach_feature_prefs has THREE policies where owner_all is FOR ALL (includes SELECT) stacked with two SELECT-only policies → multiple_permissive_policies perf warning (the same class your DB audit fought 575→510); run get_advisors after apply and consolidate. (3) Confirm coach_nutrition_prefs/client_nutrition_prefs are empty (list 0 rows) before the unconditional DROP, since merge_branch replays all history. (4) Treat null/unknown preset as 'basico' in the resolver and Zod-validate preset+domain in the write action (column has no CHECK).

improvements.title: Onboarding question: keep it to one, and make it skippable with a safe default
improvements.detail: The single-question preset selector ('¿qué tan a fondo trabajas la nutrición?') is good (research: 1-2 questions max). Two refinements: (a) make it dismissible — a coach mid-flow shouldn't be blocked; default to Intermedio (not Básico) on skip so you don't strip the 'le meto un poco' middle coach, and (b) surface it contextually the first time they open the nutrition hub, not as a global signup gate, so it has correct information scent.

roles.biggestRisk: Shipping the data layer (resolver + migration) before the consolidation/IA work, and with the default=Básico backfill, will VISIBLY remove nutrition surfaces from coaches who use them today while the menu clutter the founder actually asked to fix stays untouched — i.e. you deliver a regression dressed as a feature. The combination of the silent alumno-read RLS bug (P0) and a blanket Básico default for existing coaches (P1) means current coaches' apps get quietly poorer with no announcement, no usage-based grandfathering, and no fallback, which is exactly the 'must not break things current coaches rely on' constraint being violated. Sequence it: grandfather existing coaches from observed usage, fix the RLS predicate, and pair the visibility system with the nav consolidation so coaches perceive a cleaner app, not a stripped one.
missing: No consolidation IA/spec for the founder's actual #1 goal: the responsive/mobile menu clutter (worst surface, web bottom bar) is described in the audit but absent from the plan's deliverables.
No Ejercicios→Programas task despite it being an explicit founder requirement (button location, route-keep-alive, nav-test updates).
No nav-registry 'preference' hook design — the plan can't hide an entitled top-level menu (e.g. Nutrición for a training-only coach), which goal (2) implies.

No grandfathering/backfill strategy for EXISTING coaches: absent-row → current-behavior, usage-derived seed preset, and an explicit 'is this default change retroactive?' decision (research's #1 don't-break-users rule).
No empty-state / dependent-widget gating contract: disabling nutrition must also gate MacroBar/ComplianceRing on dashboard + client-detail, and on-but-empty surfaces need guided empty states.
No mobile-app (apps/mobile) parity plan: the Expo coach tabs (10 screens, PRIMARY_TABS + Más) speak PostgREST directly and will need the same resolver + the same RLS — the plan is web-only.

No cross-surface coherence note: /coach/foods, /coach/recipes (dead redirect), /coach/templates (dead redirect), and the duplicated Alimentos/Recetas tabs should be reconciled in the same consolidation pass.
No QA note that nav-title E2E specs and coach-nav unit tests will break when NAV_MODULES changes — they must be updated in the same change.

No accessibility/labeling spec for the new toggle list (badge Base/Pro as text not color-only, tooltip per row, lock affordance as a real CTA) — research flags color-only and dead toggles as concrete pitfalls.

roles.verdict: CONDITIONAL GO. Strategically this is the single highest-leverage feature in the EVA roadmap from a sales lens: it closes the #1 competitive gap versus Everfit (per-client overrides) and turns "menu clutter" into a demoable differentiator. But it must NOT ship as written — there are correctness landmines (RLS identity, silent grandfather loss) that, if they fire, become churn and refund events on exactly the coaches I would be upselling. Ship the consolidation (Ejercicios-into-Programas + Opciones hub) FIRST as a low-risk quick win, gate the prefs system behind a backfill + the locked-Pro-upsell UX, and only then put it in a sales deck.

topConcerns.title: Silent grandfather loss = the churn event I cannot defend
topConcerns.detail: From the money lens this is the #1 risk. The research is unambiguous and multi-source: a new column/jsonb default does NOT back-populate existing rows. If the resolver treats 'no prefs row' or 'missing section key' as OFF, every coach who relies on a base function today (a Pro coach using Nutrition, a coach showing macros to alumnos) wakes up to features that vanished from their app AND their alumnos' apps overnight. That is not a bug ticket, it is a trust-breaking, refund-demanding, public-complaint (SERNAC) event on paying customers. The plan's 'apagar = ocultar nunca borrar' protects data but does NOT protect VISIBILITY default. Required: backfill explicit rows derived from observed usage (has nutrition_meal_logs/plans -> enabled), and resolver merges missing keys to the SAFE/current value, never OFF.

topConcerns.title: RLS alumno-read predicate must be byte-identical to the proven bodycomp policy
topConcerns.detail: The audit flagged client_id = auth.uid() as likely broken. Confirmed in-repo: EVA's legacy identity is clients.id = auth.uid() (bodycomp policy 20260613120000 uses exactly client_id = (select auth.uid()) where client_id stores clients.id). If the new client_feature_prefs/coach_feature_prefs read predicates drift even slightly, the alumno silently reads ZERO prefs -> every optional section renders OFF for every alumno regardless of the coach toggle. Sales impact: the coach configures their alumnos' app, it looks correct on the coach side, and the alumno sees something different. That 'it works for me but not my client' inconsistency is the worst possible demo failure. Copy the bodycomp predicate verbatim; add an RLS isolation test asserting an alumno reads their coach's prefs.

topConcerns.title: Pro shown-locked-with-upsell is a revenue surface and must be correct, not vanish
topConcerns.detail: Decision is locked that Pro sections show locked with upsell. Research (Beehiiv/Stigg) confirms: un-entitled = locked upsell card (revenue driver), entitled-but-off = plain toggle (preference). The resolver MUST distinguish these two states and MUST consume the REAL entitlement path (hasExchangesModuleForClientContext with team-pool-wins + EVA_DISABLED_MODULES kill-switch), not a bare resolveProEntitlement(coachId). If a team-pool alumno gets wrong Pro gating, I either show an upsell to someone who already paid (support ticket) or hide an upsell from someone who would convert (lost revenue). This is the feature's ROI; getting it wrong kills the business case.

topConcerns.title: Naming collision will generate support load and erode the demo
topConcerns.detail: Three things now compete for the word module/funcion: enabled_modules (paid entitlement, read-only), the existing /coach/settings/modules read-only catalog, and the NEW 'Funciones' toggles. The existing /coach/settings is ALREADY titled 'Opciones' in team mode. A coach who confuses 'I toggled Funciones off' with 'I lost my paid module' will email support or churn. Pick distinct, plain-language names (research: avoid cute labels, match the user's mental model) and put Entitlement (read-only, what you bought) and Preference (toggle, what you show) as two clearly-labeled zones with a one-line explainer.

topConcerns.title: Per-client config does not scale by hand — must ship preset/cohort defaults
topConcerns.detail: Everfit's own model and the Trainerize idea-forum demand both prove operators will NOT hand-tune each alumno. The locked decision (3 presets basico/intermedio/profesional, per-coach AND per-client) is right, but the plan must implement the resolution cascade per-client-override -> cohort/preset default -> coach default, and decide explicitly whether changing a coach default is retroactive to existing alumnos (GitLab's lesson: don't silently auto-rewrite). Without defaults that auto-apply to NEW alumnos, the feature is unsellable for a 300-alumno team like Movida.

topConcerns.title: Mobile clutter fix is the actual sellable headline — don't bury it
topConcerns.detail: The web responsive bottom bar (8-10 items in one horizontal scroll, half off-screen) is the real pain a prospect feels in a demo on a phone. The native app already solved it (4 primary + Mas). The plan should explicitly adopt the 4-primary + 'Mas' overflow pattern on web responsive. This is the visible 'wow' that sells the whole initiative; the prefs backend is the moat but the decluttered phone nav is the pitch.

improvements.title: Ship consolidation as Phase 0 quick-win, decoupled from the prefs DB work
improvements.detail: Ejercicios-into-Programas is confirmed zero-inbound-link and low risk: drop the exercises entry from NAV_MODULES, keep /coach/exercises route alive (bookmarks/deep links/the client app /c/[slug]/exercises), add a different-colored 'Lista de ejercicios' button on the Programas LibraryHeader. Update coach-nav.test.ts and the nav-title E2E specs (collectNavTitles asserts titles) in the same change. This ships value next week with no migration risk and lets sales demo a cleaner menu immediately while the prefs system bakes.

improvements.title: Make the backfill an explicit, batched, idempotent migration step
improvements.detail: Per CLAUDE.md merge_branch replays all history, so the backfill must be forward-only/idempotent. Separate schema (add tables) from data (backfill rows). Derive each coach's initial 'enabled' set from observed usage signals (existing plans/logs per domain) so grandfathered coaches keep exactly what they have today. Confirm coach_nutrition_prefs/client_nutrition_prefs are 0-row before the unconditional DROP (list_tables), and regen database.types.ts after.

improvements.title: Resolver must take resource context, not bare coachId, and thread the kill-switch
improvements.detail: Reuse findPlanModuleContext + hasExchangesModuleForClientContext + isModuleKilledByOperator verbatim from entitlements.service.ts / nutrition-exchanges.service.ts. Effective rule per section: visible = ENTITLED(context-aware, pool-wins, not killed) AND ENABLED(coach pref) AND (client override). Preference only narrows. Treat null/unknown preset as 'basico' and Zod-validate preset+domain in the write action so a bad preset never indexes to undefined -> silent OFF.

improvements.title: Gate dependent dashboard widgets together with their module
improvements.detail: Research (NN/g + competitor cascade pitfall) plus EVA's own MacroBar/ComplianceRing consuming nutrition: if a coach disables nutrition for an alumno, the macro tile and compliance ring on the alumno dashboard must hide too, not render blank. Add this to the renderer contract: disabling a section hides the section AND its dependent widgets; on-but-empty surfaces get a guided empty state, never a blank.

improvements.title: Consolidate the 3 permissive RLS policies and run get_advisors
improvements.detail: coach_feature_prefs has owner_all (FOR ALL, includes SELECT) + client_read + team_read = stacked permissive SELECT policies, the exact multiple_permissive_policies perf class the DB audit fought down 575->510. Consolidate into fewer policies and run get_advisors (security+perf, 0 critical) before merge. Rename nutrition_set_updated_at -> generic feature_prefs_set_updated_at (or reuse an existing generic set_updated_at).

improvements.title: Add a 'preview as alumno' affordance for the coach
improvements.detail: Sales/retention lever: the #1 anxiety a coach has when toggling their alumnos' app is 'what will MY client actually see?'. /coach/settings/preview already exists. Wire the Funciones zone to it so the coach can preview the resolved alumno view per preset. This converts a scary toggle into a confident, demoable control and cuts support tickets.

roles.biggestRisk: Silent grandfather loss on paying coaches. The model 'visible = ENTITLED AND ENABLED' is correct in theory, but if ENABLED defaults to false for the existing population (because a new jsonb/column default does not back-populate old rows, and 'missing section key' resolves to OFF), then on deploy day every current coach — including Pro coaches whose Nutrition I just upsold — loses features from their app AND their alumnos' apps with no action on their part. That is the precise scenario that converts the platform's most valuable, most-engaged customers into refund-and-churn-and-SERNAC cases. The entire initiative's upside (closing the Everfit gap, cleaner UX) is wiped out by one bad default. Mitigation is non-negotiable: explicit usage-derived backfill so the migration is invisible to anyone already using a feature, plus the resolver merging missing keys to the current/safe value, never to OFF.
missing: No retroactivity decision: when a coach later changes a default/preset, does it rewrite existing alumnos or only apply to new ones? GitLab's lesson is to make this an explicit operator choice, not a silent cascade. Plan is silent on this.

No cohort/group default layer between coach-default and per-client-override. Everfit's gold-standard model is default -> group (Autoflow) -> per-client. With only per-coach + per-client, a 300-alumno team (Movida) cannot bulk-configure a subset; per-client-by-hand does not scale.

No mobile-app (apps/mobile, PostgREST-direct) parity plan. The new tables and the nav consolidation must reflect in the Expo app too — apps/mobile talks PostgREST directly, so the RLS on the new tables and any client_feature_prefs reads must work for the native client, and the 10 registered tabs / PRIMARY_TABS need the same Ejercicios demotion.

No analytics/telemetry on what coaches actually disable. Sales/product needs to know which functions get turned off most (signals a confusing or low-value feature) and which preset wins. Ship lightweight tracking from day one to inform pricing and the upsell.

No explicit empty-state copy spec. Research is emphatic: on-but-empty must guide ('registra una comida para ver tus macros'), off must hide. No content spec exists for the alumno-facing empty/guided states.

No rollback/kill-switch for the prefs layer itself. If the resolver misbehaves in prod, there is no documented flag to bypass prefs and fall back to pure-entitlement visibility. Given the grandfather risk, an Edge Config kill-switch (fail-open to current behavior) is warranted.

roles.role: Senior Backend / Staff Engineer reviewing the menu-consolidation + feature-visibility PLAN through a "don't break current coaches" lens (acting as SDR review).
roles.verdict: Strong, well-researched plan with a correct core model (visible = ENTITLED AND NOT killed AND ENABLED; preference only narrows; hide-never-delete). The architecture (side tables + pure config package + single server resolver) is the right shape and reuses EVA's existing patterns. APPROVE the direction, but DO NOT ship F0 as-is: the on-disk migration has real backend gaps (out-of-band table drift, dropped preset CHECK, generic-system naming bound to "nutrition", missing usage-based backfill for existing coaches, and resolver signature that ignores team-pool/kill-switch). Fix these before apply. NOTE: the prior gap-analysis's #1 "blocking" RLS bug is a FALSE ALARM — EVA uses legacy identity clients.id = auth.uid(), so the migration's client-read policies are correct as written.

topConcerns.title: Out-of-band table drift: the tables being DROPped were never created by a migration
topConcerns.detail: The PLAN says coach_nutrition_prefs/client_nutrition_prefs are 'empty tables applied by mistake.' Grep of supabase/migrations shows NO migration ever CREATEd them — they exist only because someone ran raw SQL against prod outside the migration history. The new migration's `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS` is a no-op on a fresh replay (merge_branch replays ALL history forward-only), so the drift is silently 'fixed' on replay but the LIVE prod DB has objects the migration ledger doesn't know about. Before applying F0: confirm via list_tables that those two tables (a) exist in live prod and (b) have 0 rows, and document that this DROP is reconciling out-of-band state, not migrated state. If they ever held data, the DROP is silent data loss. This is the kind of ledger/reality divergence that bites later schema work.

topConcerns.title: No backfill / grandfather strategy for EXISTING coaches — the core 'don't break current coaches' risk
topConcerns.detail: Default global = Basico (everything optional OFF). The resolver treats 'no prefs row' as preset=basico => every advanced nutrition section a current coach is ALREADY using (micros, plate, recipes, off-plan log, notes) silently DISAPPEARS from their app and their alumnos' app on deploy. June-2026 research is unanimous: a column/row default does NOT grandfather existing users; you must explicitly backfill. EVA already ships nutrition sections live today. Required: a one-shot, idempotent, batched backfill that derives each existing coach's preset/section overrides from OBSERVED USAGE (has nutrition_meal_logs / non-empty templates / micros configured => enable those sections; e.g. seed preset='intermedio' or write explicit section overrides) — NOT a blanket basico. Without this, F0 is a visible regression for every nutrition-active coach the moment it lands. The PLAN's own §5 invariant 'defaults apply only to NEW clients, never retroactive' is contradicted by shipping basico-by-absence to existing coaches.

topConcerns.title: Resolver signature ignores team-pool entitlement context and the operator kill-switch
topConcerns.detail: The PLAN model line says 'NOT killed (operador)' but the resolver pseudocode is resolveFeaturePrefs(coachId, clientId?) reducing entitlement to resolveProEntitlement(coachId). Real EVA entitlement resolves by RESOURCE CONTEXT: hasModule()/hasExchangesModuleForClientContext picks team pool (teams.enabled_modules via clientTeamId) over coaches.enabled_modules, and applyOperatorKillSwitch/isModuleKilledByOperator (EVA_DISABLED_MODULES) sits above tenant entitlement. If the resolver only reads coaches.enabled_modules by coachId, team-pool alumnos get WRONG Pro gating (a Movida-style team where the module lives on teams, not the coach, would show locked or wrongly-open). Must thread the same context the existing code uses (planId or {coachId, clientTeamId, clientOrgId}) and reuse findPlanModuleContext + hasExchangesModuleForClientContext + the kill-switch verbatim — do not reimplement entitlement.

topConcerns.title: 'Generic' system, but the migration is hard-bound to nutrition naming
topConcerns.detail: The trigger function is public.nutrition_set_updated_at() on tables named *_feature_prefs that are explicitly meant to host 'training' and every future domain. This contradicts the genericity thesis and will confuse the next dev who adds the 'training' domain. Rename to feature_prefs_set_updated_at (grep first for an existing generic set_updated_at to reuse). Cheap now, annoying to rename after rows exist and other domains depend on it.

topConcerns.title: Permissive-policy stacking re-introduces the perf debt the DB audit just paid down
topConcerns.detail: coach_feature_prefs has THREE policies where owner_all is FOR ALL (which includes SELECT) plus two SELECT-only policies (client_read, team_read). Postgres evaluates all permissive SELECT policies per row (OR'd) — this is exactly the multiple_permissive_policies pattern the 2026-06 DB audit drove from 575 down to 510. client_feature_prefs has the same shape (coach_all FOR ALL + client_read + team_all FOR ALL). Run get_advisors after apply; if flagged, split FOR ALL into explicit INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policies so SELECT has fewer overlapping permissive policies. These tables are read on every nutrition render (coach ficha + alumno app), so this is a hot path.

topConcerns.title: preset CHECK constraint was dropped vs the design doc, with validation punted entirely to 'the app'
topConcerns.detail: The design doc §7b specifies CHECK (preset in ('basico','intermedio','profesional')). The on-disk migration drops it ('valida la app') for genericity. That's a defensible generic-storage choice ONLY IF two things are guaranteed: (1) the resolver treats null/unknown preset as 'basico' (safe default) so s.presets[preset] never indexes undefined => section silently OFF; (2) the write-path server action Zod-validates preset+domain. Neither is stated as a hard requirement in F0/F1. Make both explicit acceptance criteria, or a typo'd preset write turns every optional section off for that coach with no error.

improvements.title: Add F0.5 'Backfill existing coaches' as a gated, separate migration with usage-derived presets
improvements.detail: Separate the schema migration from the data backfill (never UPDATE inside the DDL migration). Batched, throttled, idempotent (re-runnable since merge_branch replays). Logic: for each coach, inspect nutrition usage (any nutrition_meal_logs, any plan with micros/plate/recipes configured) and write an explicit coach_feature_prefs row enabling the sections they already use — derive preset from signal, default to 'intermedio' (not 'basico') for any coach with an active nutrition template so current behavior is preserved. Coaches with zero nutrition usage can land on 'basico' safely. Make this a hard gate: F0 schema cannot deploy to prod-facing coaches without the backfill in the same release.

improvements.title: Thread resource context + kill-switch into the resolver; reuse existing entitlement code
improvements.detail: Change signature to resolveFeaturePrefs({coachId, clientId?, clientTeamId?, clientOrgId?, planId?}, domain). For requiresModule rows call hasExchangesModuleForClientContext / hasModule (which already does pool-wins + isModuleKilledByOperator), not a new resolveProEntitlement(coachId). This guarantees team-pool alumnos (Movida-style) gate correctly and the EVA_DISABLED_MODULES kill-switch still wins. Wrap in React.cache like getNutritionProEnabledForClient.

improvements.title: Confirm the RLS predicates are correct (they ARE) and add the team-write parity the PLAN implies
improvements.detail: VERIFIED against migration 20260613120000 + 20260517120000: EVA identity is legacy clients.id = auth.uid() (there is no clients.user_id). So coach_feature_prefs_client_read (c.id = auth.uid()) and client_feature_prefs_client_read (client_id = auth.uid()) are CORRECT — the prior gap-analysis flagging them as a P0 break is wrong; do not 'fix' them to user_id (that column doesn't exist and would break reads). current_user_pool_client_ids/current_user_pool_coach_ids helpers exist and are the right InitPlan-safe pattern. One real gap: client_feature_prefs has a team_all (pool coach can override per-client) but coach_feature_prefs has only client_read for pool, no team write — confirm that's intentional (pool coaches edit per-client overrides but the team-level coach prefs are owner-only). Add a tx-rollback RLS test (SET LOCAL ROLE authenticated + jwt.claims) for: coach reads/writes own; alumno reads only own coach's row; pool coach reads peer; alumno CANNOT write coach_feature_prefs.

improvements.title: Reframe 'new Opciones hub' as a restructure of the EXISTING /coach/settings (already titled 'Opciones')
improvements.detail: settings/page.tsx metadata title is already 'Opciones', and nav key settings_team already routes /coach/settings as 'Opciones' in team context; the team branch already renders a 3-card hub (the exact pattern wanted). So this is NOT greenfield. Decide explicitly: does 'Mi Marca' (standalone /coach/settings body = BrandSettingsForm) move to /coach/settings/brand so the hub becomes the parent, while 'Suscripcion' stays at /coach/subscription? Resolve the name clash: the page is called 'Opciones' and the new button is 'Opciones Coach' — pick distinct labels (e.g. hub = 'Opciones', new button = 'Funciones' or 'Personalizacion') or users/devs will conflate them. Also relate the new 'Funciones' (preference toggles) to the existing read-only /coach/settings/modules (ENTITLEMENT catalog) with a one-line explainer so coaches don't confuse 'what I bought' with 'what I show'.

improvements.title: Route the Ejercicios->Programas consolidation through the typed nav registry + its tests, keep the route alive
improvements.detail: Audit confirms /coach/exercises has ZERO inbound in-app links (only NAV_MODULES) — clean to demote. But: (1) keep the /coach/exercises route reachable (bookmarks, /c/[coach_slug]/exercises client app exists); only remove the registry entry. (2) coach-nav.ts is pure + unit-tested and E2E specs assert nav titles (collectNavTitles, aside nav a[title]) — removing the entry WILL break coach-nav.test.ts and nav-title E2E; update them in the same change. (3) Add the 'Lista de ejercicios' button as variant=outline/secondary in LibraryHeader.tsx next to the existing 'Nueva plantilla' action. (4) Mirror in apps/mobile: drop 'ejercicios' from the CoachMobileChrome overflow and the (tabs) registration intent, leaving the screen file intact.

improvements.title: Fix worst clutter spot (web responsive bottom bar) with a 4-primary + 'Mas' pattern, reuse mobile's PRIMARY_TABS
improvements.detail: The single biggest clutter source is NOT desktop — it's the web responsive bottom bar in CoachSidebar.tsx (lines ~188-306): 8-10 flat horizontally-scrolling tiles, ~half off-screen behind scroll chevrons, no overflow grouping. apps/mobile already solved this (PRIMARY_TABS = home/clientes/builder/nutricion + 'Mas' overflow). Port that pattern to web: ~4 primary (Inicio/Alumnos/Planes/Nutri) + 'Mas' sheet hosting Opciones/Soporte/modules. splitNavItems already gives core vs modules to drive grouping. This + the Opciones hub + Ejercicios fold takes web mobile from 8-10 flat tiles to 5 clear ones. Each entitled module currently appends another tile to that flat scroll — so the prefs ENABLE toggle is ALSO a declutter lever (disabling a module removes its tile).

improvements.title: Add a nav-level featurePref hook if base menus are to be coach-toggleable
improvements.detail: Founder goal (2) is prefs over base FUNCTIONS including nav. Today getVisibleNavItems knows only `entitlement`, not `preference`. If a coach should be able to hide an ENTITLED base menu (e.g. hide Nutricion even on Pro), add a parallel `featurePref?: {domain, sectionKey}` hook in NAV_MODULES that AND-narrows after contexts+entitlement — same most-specific-wins model. If nav-level toggling is out of scope for v1 (only in-page section toggles), state that explicitly so it isn't silently assumed.

improvements.title: Add a catalog-style cross-check test + governance to prevent re-bloat
