improvements.detail: Mirror the MODULE_CATALOG_KEYS vs MODULE_KEYS catalog.test.ts discipline: a test asserting every NutritionSection with requiresModule references a real ModuleKey, every preset maps to a defined section, and core sections are never toggleable. Codify the governance rule (new section => row in FeatureSections or PR rejected) as a CONTRIBUTING/PR-checklist item, plus a hard rule: a Funcion toggle writes ONLY to *_feature_prefs.sections jsonb and is FORBIDDEN to delete/null any nutrition_* row (guards the ON DELETE CASCADE data-loss path).

roles.biggestRisk: Silently regressing CURRENT nutrition-active coaches on deploy. Because the resolver treats 'no prefs row' as preset=basico (all optional sections OFF), and EVA already ships micros/plate/recipes/off-plan/notes live, the moment F0 lands every coach who is using those features today — and all their alumnos — loses them from the UI with no warning, no migration, no opt-in. The data is preserved (hide-not-delete is correct), but the experience visibly breaks for the exact users the founder said must NOT break. The fix is non-negotiable: a usage-derived backfill that grandfathers existing coaches into the sections they already use (default existing nutrition users to 'intermedio'+explicit overrides, not 'basico'), shipped in the SAME release as the schema. A column/row default does not grandfather anyone — you must backfill explicitly.
missing: Backfill/grandfather plan for existing coaches (the on-disk migration creates empty tables and ships basico-by-absence — no F0.5 data migration exists). This is the top omission.

Reconciliation of the out-of-band coach_nutrition_prefs/client_nutrition_prefs tables: no migration ever created them, so the ledger and live prod have diverged; the plan should verify (list_tables, 0 rows) and document the DROP as drift reconciliation before apply.

Empty-state vs hidden-state UX rule: research is explicit — module OFF => hide nav entry AND dependent dashboard widgets together (MacroBar/ComplianceRing consume nutrition; gate them with the same flag), but on-but-empty surfaces get guided empty states, never blanks. The plan gates the nutrition tab but doesn't list the dependent dashboard widgets (ComplianceScoresCard, MacroBar, NutritionDailySummary, heroComplianceBundle — all in the current diff) that must be gated by the same resolver.
Operator kill-switch (EVA_DISABLED_MODULES) and team-pool entitlement path in the resolver signature — stated in the model line but absent from the resolver pseudocode.

apps/mobile parity: the prefs resolver and the Ejercicios demotion must be mirrored in the Expo app (it talks PostgREST directly and has its own tab registry); the plan is web-only. Cross-surface nav drift (mobile has check-ins/perfil tabs web lacks; web has team/cardio/movement mobile lacks) is unaddressed.
RLS tx-rollback test (SET LOCAL ROLE authenticated + jwt.claims) and get_advisors run after apply — required by EVA's own 'validate before prod' rule and to catch the permissive-policy stacking.
Decision on whether nav-level (top-level menu) visibility is coach-toggleable; getVisibleNavItems has no preference hook today, and founder goal (2) implies base-function toggling.
Generic-naming cleanup: nutrition_set_updated_at trigger on generic *_feature_prefs tables contradicts the 'extensible to all domains' design goal.
Zod validation of preset+domain on the write path and resolver's null-preset => 'basico' fallback (CHECK constraint was dropped, validation punted to 'the app' but not specified).

roles.verdict: APPROVE WITH CHANGES. The plan is strategically right and unusually well-grounded for CS: it consolidates clutter, separates entitlement (billing) from preference (UX), and hard-codes hide-don't-delete. From a "don't break current coaches" lens it is mostly safe BECAUSE preference only narrows and core is always ON. But there are two CS-grade landmines the plan under-treats: (1) the migration FLIPS advanced nutrition sections from visible-today to OFF-by-default with NO grandfather/backfill, which is a silent regression for any coach already using micros/plate/recipes; and (2) the "Opciones" hub is described as greenfield when /coach/settings is already titled "Opciones" and already a card-hub — a sloppy rename here breaks muscle memory and existing nav/E2E. Note: the audit's "BLOCKING" RLS bug (#1, client_read using clients.id=auth.uid()) is a FALSE ALARM — I verified clients.id IS the auth.uid in EVA (foods/exercises/client_consents self-policies all use it), so the migration's client-read policies are correct as written.

topConcerns.title: Silent regression: advanced sections go OFF-by-default for coaches already using them, with no grandfather/backfill
topConcerns.detail: This is the #1 'we broke current coaches' risk. F0 says 'las secciones avanzadas pasan a OFF por default ya' and F1 sets existing coaches to preset Basico. A coach using a coach_feature_prefs row that does not exist yet resolves to s.presets['basico'] = OFF. So a nutritionist already showing micros/plate/recipes to her alumnos will open the app post-deploy and find those sections VANISHED from both her ficha and every alumno app — looks like data loss even though it is render-only. The research you commissioned names this exact pitfall: a column/preset default does NOT back-populate existing rows; you MUST backfill from observed usage. Fix: a one-time backfill that derives each existing coach's preset from real signals (has any plan with micros populated / recipes / off-plan logs -> seed 'intermedio' or 'profesional'; truly plan-only -> 'basico'), OR resolve 'no row' to a grandfather preset that preserves today's everything-visible behavior. This MUST ship in the same migration/phase, not later.

topConcerns.title: 'Opciones' hub naming collision + nav/E2E breakage for existing coaches
topConcerns.detail: /coach/settings already has metadata title 'Opciones' and in team context already renders a 3-card hub. The plan treats the hub as NEW and introduces a button literally named 'Opciones Coach' inside a page already called 'Opciones'. For a coach who today taps 'Mi Marca' to reach branding, a restructure that moves branding behind a hub changes a daily path. CS impact: confusion tickets, 'where did my branding go'. Also coach-nav.ts is unit-tested and E2E specs assert nav titles (collectNavTitles / aside nav a[title]) — removing/renaming brand+billing+settings_team entries breaks those tests if not updated in the same change. Fix: explicitly frame this as a restructure of existing /coach/settings + /coach/subscription, pick non-colliding names (e.g. hub='Opciones', third button='Funciones' or 'Preferencias' not 'Opciones Coach'), keep deep links alive, and update coach-nav tests + E2E in the same PR.

topConcerns.title: Three stacked permissive SELECT policies per table -> perf-advisor regression risk
topConcerns.detail: coach_feature_prefs has owner_all (FOR ALL, which includes SELECT) + client_read (SELECT) + team_read (SELECT) = three permissive policies evaluated on every read. This is exactly the multiple_permissive_policies class your DB audit fought down from 575->510. client_feature_prefs similarly has coach_all (FOR ALL) + client_read + team_all (FOR ALL) stacking. Each read OR-evaluates all matching policies. Fix: run get_advisors after apply; consider consolidating SELECT into one policy with an OR predicate, and keep write (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) in a separate restricted policy rather than FOR ALL, so a SELECT does not drag a WITH CHECK-bearing FOR ALL into the plan.

topConcerns.title: Dependent widgets (dashboard MacroBar / ComplianceRing) may render when their section is toggled OFF
topConcerns.detail: The competitor research flagged this precisely: disabling a parent (nutrition section) while a dependent surface still renders = inconsistent, broken-looking UI for the alumno. EVA's dashboard MacroBar and ComplianceRing consume nutrition and are being touched in this very branch. If the resolver gates only the nutrition tab/shell but not the dashboard cards, an alumno of a 'Basico' coach could still see a macro ring on the dashboard while the nutrition detail is hidden. Fix: the F2 wiring checklist must explicitly enumerate EVERY surface that consumes a gated section (dashboard cards, compliance bundle, hero) and gate them from the same resolver, not just NutritionShell/NutritionTabB5.

topConcerns.title: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS on the empty nutrition_prefs tables is unconditional and replays on every merge
topConcerns.detail: Lines 6-7 DROP coach_nutrition_prefs / client_nutrition_prefs with no emptiness guard. CLAUDE.md says merge_branch replays ALL history forward-only. If those tables ever acquire data (or someone re-creates them), this silently destroys it on every future merge. Low real risk today (you assert empty) but cheap to harden: confirm 0 rows via list_tables before shipping, and add a guard/comment so a future dev does not resurrect-and-drop.

improvements.title: Add an explicit grandfather/backfill step to F0 with a coach-visible 'we kept your settings' cue
improvements.detail: Beyond the technical backfill (P0): from a CS angle, even a correct backfill should be COMMUNICATED. When a coach first opens Opciones Coach post-deploy, show a one-line banner: 'Detectamos que ya usabas micros y recetas — los dejamos encendidos. Ajustalos cuando quieras.' This turns a scary silent change into a trust-building moment and pre-empts support tickets. Derive the seeded preset from nutrition_meal_logs / plan micro fields / recipe usage, exactly as the research's 'infer from usage signals' guidance prescribes.

improvements.title: Make 'no prefs row' fail OPEN to today's behavior during the transition, then converge
improvements.detail: Safest rollout: ship the resolver so that a missing coach_feature_prefs row resolves to 'show everything currently shown' (grandfather), run the backfill to materialize explicit rows, and only AFTER backfill completes flip the default-for-new-coaches to 'basico'. This guarantees zero existing coach sees a section disappear, while new coaches get the clean Basico default. Asymmetric defaults (existing=ON, new=OFF) is the canonical grandfathering shape from your own research.

improvements.title: Rename the trigger function to be domain-neutral
improvements.detail: The function is public.nutrition_set_updated_at() but the tables are generic *_feature_prefs covering training/etc later. It contradicts the genericity thesis and will confuse the next dev wiring a 'training' domain. Rename to feature_prefs_set_updated_at (or grep for an existing generic set_updated_at and reuse). Cosmetic but cheap and the plan explicitly sells genericity.

improvements.title: Reuse MODULE_CATALOG copy for Pro badges/upsell instead of re-authoring strings
improvements.detail: The locked Pro rows (nutrition_exchanges) already have catalog copy, pitch, and surfaces in @eva/module-catalog driving the existing read-only /coach/settings/modules page. The new 'Funciones' zone should consume that same copy for the 🔒 upsell, so Modulos (entitlement, read-only) and Funciones (preference, toggles) tell one consistent story. Add a one-line in-UI explainer distinguishing the two — coaches WILL confuse 'modulo' (paid) vs 'funcion' (free toggle); the shared word is a support-ticket generator.

improvements.title: Add the catalog-parity test the plan mentions but does not enforce
improvements.detail: The plan says NUTRITION_SECTIONS mirrors MODULE_CATALOG and governance rejects PRs that add a section without a row. Make that a real test (catalog.test.ts-style) asserting every section with requiresModule references a valid ModuleKey, and that every preset maps every key — otherwise an unknown/null preset indexes presets[preset] = undefined and the section silently goes OFF (your own audit's gap #2). Also Zod-validate preset+domain in the write action and treat unknown preset as 'basico'.

improvements.title: Resolver must take resource context (team/org/plan), not bare coachId, to gate pool alumnos correctly
improvements.detail: resolveNutritionPrefs(coachId, clientId?) reduces entitlement to resolveProEntitlement(coachId). But real Pro entitlement resolves by resource context — team pool wins via clientTeamId, then plan coach (hasExchangesModuleForClientContext + findPlanModuleContext) — and applies the EVA_DISABLED_MODULES operator kill-switch. Thread the same context the existing code uses and reuse those functions verbatim; otherwise a team-pool alumno gets wrong Pro gating (sees locked, or worse, a Pro section the coach is not entitled to).

roles.biggestRisk: A nutritionist or nutrition-heavy coach who is actively using micros/plate/recipes TODAY opens EVA after this ships and finds those sections gone from her ficha and from every one of her alumnos' apps — because the OFF-by-default preset applies to coaches with no prefs row and there is no grandfather/backfill. It is render-only (data is safe), but to the coach AND her paying alumnos it reads as 'EVA deleted my nutrition work,' which is the single highest-churn-risk perception for a CS team to clean up. The fix (backfill preset from observed usage + fail-open during transition) is cheap and MUST be in the same phase as the default flip — never a follow-up.
missing: No grandfather/backfill strategy for EXISTING coaches/alumnos — the plan flips defaults to OFF with no plan to preserve current visibility (the core 'don't break coaches' gap).
No rollout/communication plan: no in-app cue telling coaches their settings were preserved, no changelog, no support-team heads-up. CS will field 'where did nutrition go' tickets blind.

No reversibility/kill-switch for the visibility system itself: if the resolver mis-gates in prod, what is the instant rollback? (Edge Config flag to bypass prefs and show-all would mirror EVA's existing fail-open patterns.)
No gating of DEPENDENT surfaces (dashboard MacroBar, ComplianceRing, hero compliance bundle) — only NutritionShell/NutritionTabB5 are named; dependent widgets can render orphaned.
No empty-state guidance for on-but-empty sections (alumno with section enabled but no plan/logs sees a blank, reads as broken) — research flagged this explicitly.

No mobile (apps/mobile) parity plan: the alumno RN app and coach mobile chrome read PostgREST directly and render nutrition; if they do not consume the same resolver, web and mobile alumno views diverge (one shows micros, the other does not).

No analytics/telemetry to measure adoption: which presets coaches pick, which sections get toggled — needed to validate the 3-preset assumption and to spot coaches who disabled something then complained.

No definition of what happens to a per-client override (client_feature_prefs) when a coach reassigns/changes the alumno's plan or the alumno moves team scope — stale override rows could re-narrow unexpectedly.

roles.role: Legal & Compliance Counsel (Chile) — review of EVA menu consolidation + coach/client feature-visibility & preferences plan
roles.verdict: Conditionally approvable from a legal/compliance standpoint, but NOT ready to ship as drafted. The core architecture (visible = ENTITLED ∧ ENABLED, preference only narrows, hide-never-delete) is legally sound and actually REDUCES several risks (data-minimization, SERNAC billing transparency). However the plan has concrete Ley 19.628 (datos personales) / SERNAC (Ley 19.496 consumidor) / contractual-continuity exposures that must be closed before it touches existing coaches: (1) silent feature-removal for existing coaches/alumnos with no notice or grandfathering = unilateral alteration of a contracted service; (2) a per-client toggle that lets a COACH hide an ALUMNO's own logged health/nutrition data risks blocking the data subject's access/portability rights (the coach is not the sole controller of the alumno's personal data); (3) the RLS bug flagged in the gap analysis is also a privacy-isolation defect, not just a functional one. Fix the consent/notice, data-subject-access, audit-trail, and entitlement/billing-coupling items below and it is approvable.

topConcerns.title: Silent removal of features existing coaches rely on = unilateral modification of a contracted service (Ley 19.496 / SERNAC)
topConcerns.detail: The research itself flags the #1 migration trap: an absent prefs row resolving to OFF makes existing coaches/alumnos silently lose behavior. From a Chilean consumer-law lens this is worse than a bug — for a PAYING coach, a base-plan function they have been using is part of the onboarded service. Turning it off by default (even via the 'basico' preset) without notice is an arbitrary unilateral alteration of the service the consumer contracted (Ley 19.496 art. 16/17 on abusive clauses + unilateral changes). The plan MUST grandfather existing coaches/alumnos to their current effective state derived from observed usage (has plans/logs => keep enabled), default-ON for the installed base, default-OFF only for NEW coaches. This is both the engineering fix AND the legal-continuity fix. Decision 'micros base free-but-OFF' is acceptable for NEW coaches only; for the existing base it must be free-but-ON-if-used.

topConcerns.title: Per-client (alumno) feature toggle can block the data subject's right to access/port their OWN health data (Ley 19.628 + incoming Ley 21.719)
topConcerns.detail: client_feature_prefs lets the COACH hide sections of the ALUMNO's app. Nutrition/anthropometry/check-in logs are the ALUMNO's personal (and health-category) data; the alumno is the data subject with an indfeasible/non-waivable right of access and portability (ARCO under 19.628; reinforced and enforceable with fines under Ley 21.719, the new data-protection law published 2024 with the Agencia and sanctions regime phasing in by Dec-2026). If a coach toggles 'Mi progreso' or 'Nutrición' OFF, the alumno may be unable to SEE or EXPORT data they themselves entered or that concerns their body. 'Hide-never-delete' protects retention but NOT the access right. Requirement: a coach toggle may hide a coaching SURFACE but must never remove the alumno's path to view/export their own logged data. Keep the existing Privacidad/ARCO link (CoachSidebar already renders it) reachable regardless of any client_feature_prefs row, and ensure any hidden data subject's-own-data section still has a 'mis datos / exportar' route. Document this as an invariant in the PLAN.

topConcerns.title: RLS client-read policy bug is a privacy isolation/cross-tenant defect, not only a functional one
topConcerns.detail: The gap analysis found client_feature_prefs_client_read uses client_id = auth.uid() while in EVA clients.id is NOT the auth uid (alumno maps via clients.user_id). Beyond rendering everything OFF, a wrong predicate on a prefs table is a confidentiality control: if the corrected predicate is written loosely (e.g. joining without scoping team_id/org_id), one coach's preference rows — which reveal which features/health-domains a competitor coach has configured for which alumnos — could leak across tenants. This is exactly the isolation class your DB audit and enterprise-isolation work already hardened. Before apply: copy the verified alumno-self predicate (bodycomp_student_self_select) AND run get_advisors (security+performance) on a branch; 0 criticals required per your own Supabase protocol. Treat prefs tables as in-scope for the RLS isolation test suite.

topConcerns.title: No audit trail for who toggled what / when — needed for SERNAC evidence and dispute defense
topConcerns.detail: enabled_modules/coach_addons are billing-coupled and you already keep billing_snapshots as SERNAC evidence. The NEW feature_prefs layer is free and coach-mutable, but disputes will still arise ('mi coach me ocultó/activó X', 'EVA me apagó una función que pagaba'). The jsonb sections + updated_at trigger captures only the LATEST state, no history and no actor. Add at minimum: (a) updated_by / actor on writes, (b) for the migration backfill, a one-row provenance marker distinguishing 'grandfathered from usage' vs 'new default' so you can later prove a coach was NOT silently downgraded. Without this you cannot answer a SERNAC reclamo about a feature change. Reuse the org_audit_logs / purge_audit pattern already in the schema rather than inventing a new mechanism.

topConcerns.title: Pro-locked-with-upsell surfacing must not become misleading/aggressive commercial practice
topConcerns.detail: Decision 'Pro shown locked w/ upsell' and the research-endorsed 'show, don't hide' pattern are fine, but Ley 19.496 prohibits misleading advertising and the publicidad-engañosa standard applies. Two concrete requirements: (1) the locked/upsell card must state the real recurring price (the $9.990/mo frozen price_clp and that nutrition_exchanges requires a Pro tier) — not a teaser — consistent with the precio-claro obligations you already honor in billing_snapshots; (2) respect the anti-harassment 'sold only in 2 surfaces' rule already in code (Settings>Módulos + /subscription#addons). Do NOT let the new Funciones zone become a third dark-pattern upsell surface sprinkling 'desbloquear' CTAs across every toggle. Keep the upsell to the existing entitlement catalog; Funciones toggles should be neutral on/off for entitled items and a single quiet lock for unentitled ones.

topConcerns.title: DROP TABLE in a forward-only/replayed migration is a data-destruction risk
topConcerns.detail: The migration unconditionally DROPs coach_nutrition_prefs/client_nutrition_prefs. Your own CLAUDE.md states merge_branch resets prod and re-runs ALL history, and forbids destructive DROP/rename. Even if empty today, an unconditional DROP of a personal-data-bearing table name in a replayed history is both an architecture-rule violation and, were data ever present, a Ley 19.628 unlawful-destruction exposure. Confirm 0 rows via list_tables before apply, gate the DROP defensively, and snapshot (_bak) per your protocol.

topConcerns.title: Cross-surface drift (web vs apps/mobile) can produce inconsistent disclosures of the same data subject
topConcerns.detail: apps/mobile talks PostgREST directly and has its own tab registry (check-ins, perfil) that the web nav lacks. If feature_prefs is enforced only in the web renderer and not mirrored in apps/mobile (and apps/enterprise), an alumno could see a section on one surface and not the other, or a hidden surface could still expose data on mobile. For compliance, the controlling gate must be SERVER-SIDE (resolver consumed by all clients), never client-render-only. Confirm the resolver is the single source consumed by web, mobile and enterprise — the plan currently scopes itself to web/nutrition.

improvements.title: Grandfather the installed base from observed usage, with a recorded provenance flag
improvements.detail: Backfill coach_feature_prefs/client_feature_prefs for ALL existing coaches/alumnos in the same change: derive each section's enabled state from existing data (nutrition_meals/nutrition_meal_logs/plans => ON), apply 'basico' default only to coaches with no usage. Store a column/flag (e.g. seeded_from='usage'|'default') so a later SERNAC or coach dispute can be answered with evidence. Per the research, a column default does NOT back-populate rows — an explicit batched, idempotent, forward-only backfill is mandatory (matches your merge_branch replay rule).

improvements.title: Encode the data-subject-access invariant in the PLAN and the resolver
improvements.detail: Add an explicit rule: client_feature_prefs may hide a COACHING surface but the resolver/renderer must always preserve the alumno's path to view and export their own logged data, and the Privacidad·ARCO link must render regardless of any prefs row. Add a test asserting that with all optional client sections OFF, the alumno still reaches their own-data export. This operationalizes Ley 19.628/21.719 access+portability and prevents the toggle from becoming a rights-blocker.

improvements.title: Add a notice/changelog touchpoint for any default change affecting existing users
improvements.detail: Per the GitLab precedent in the research, changing a default and rewriting existing users are separate decisions. For EVA: never auto-rewrite an existing coach's chosen prefs on future default changes; and when a feature's visibility materially changes for the installed base, surface an in-app notice (the existing News bell in CoachSidebar is the natural channel). This discharges the consumer-information duty under Ley 19.496 for service modifications.

improvements.title: Keep entitlement strictly billing-coupled; never let a free preference widen entitlement
improvements.detail: Reaffirm in code review that feature_prefs can only NARROW. The resolver must read hasModule/hasExchangesModuleForClientContext + the EVA_DISABLED_MODULES kill-switch (gap analysis #6) so a preference can never surface a paid module the coach hasn't bought. This keeps the money/entitlement boundary clean for SERNAC and avoids accidentally giving away paid modules (revenue + contractual integrity).

improvements.title: Treat the new prefs tables as in-scope for the RLS isolation test suite and advisors gate
improvements.detail: Add coach_feature_prefs/client_feature_prefs to tests/enterprise/rls-isolation.spec.ts (or the separation suite). Validate on a Supabase branch with synthetic seed as authenticated+claims (never service_role), confirm get_advisors security+performance = 0 critical, snapshot prod (_bak), then merge — exactly your documented protocol. Fix the client_id predicate by copying bodycomp_student_self_select and verify team_id/org_id scoping so no cross-tenant pref leakage.

improvements.title: Consolidate the 3 permissive SELECT policies to avoid perf regressions you already fought down
improvements.detail: As the gap analysis notes, FOR ALL owner policy + 2 SELECT-only policies stack (multiple_permissive_policies) — the same class your DB audit reduced 575->510. Consolidate so the prefs tables don't reintroduce per-row policy multiplication on a hot read path (every coach/alumno page resolves prefs).

improvements.title: Name the layers unambiguously in UI to avoid consumer confusion (consumer-clarity duty)
improvements.detail: Settings>Módulos = ENTITLEMENT (what you bought, read-only, billing). New Funciones zone = PREFERENCE (what you show, free toggles). The word 'módulo/función' is overloaded. Clear, plain-language labels (research warns against cute labels) reduce the chance a coach believes turning a Funcion OFF cancels a paid module (or vice-versa) — which would itself generate billing reclamos.

roles.biggestRisk: A per-client (alumno) toggle that, combined with default-OFF resolution, lets a coach (or an EVA default change) silently switch off a surface through which the ALUMNO accesses or exports their OWN health/nutrition personal data — simultaneously (a) impairing the data subject's non-waivable access/portability rights under Ley 19.628 and the soon-enforceable Ley 21.719 (Agencia + fines phasing in by end-2026, with health data treated as sensitive), and (b) for paying coaches, unilaterally altering a contracted service without notice or grandfathering under Ley 19.496/SERNAC. The hide-never-delete invariant protects RETENTION but does nothing for ACCESS — that gap is the single largest legal exposure and must be closed with a server-side invariant that preserves the alumno's own-data view/export path plus usage-based grandfathering of the installed base.
missing: No grandfathering/notice strategy for existing PAYING coaches whose base-plan functions get defaulted OFF — unilateral service modification under Ley 19.496/SERNAC; the plan treats the migration as greenfield.

No statement that the alumno's right to access/export their OWN logged data survives any client_feature_prefs toggle (Ley 19.628 ARCO / Ley 21.719); hide-never-delete covers retention but not access/portability.

No audit/provenance trail (actor + history + 'grandfathered vs default' flag) on the new free, coach-mutable prefs layer — cannot evidence a SERNAC reclamo about who changed a feature; org_audit_logs pattern not reused.
Ley 21.719 (new Chilean data-protection law, sensitive=health, Agencia + sanctions by ~Dec-2026) is not referenced at all despite the plan touching health-category data subject rights.
Server-side single-source enforcement across ALL surfaces (web + apps/mobile PostgREST + apps/enterprise) is unconfirmed; risk of inconsistent disclosure of the same data subject's data per surface.

DPA/controller-processor clarity: when a COACH hides an ALUMNO's data surface, who is controller vs processor of that personal data is undefined — matters for which party answers an ARCO/access request and for EVA's own liability allocation.

No record-of-processing / data-map update for the two new personal-data-bearing tables (coach_feature_prefs references coaches; client_feature_prefs references alumnos and effectively encodes which health domains are active per data subject).

Unconditional DROP TABLE of personal-data-named tables in a replayed forward-only migration violates your own destructive-DDL rule and is an unlawful-destruction risk if ever populated; no _bak snapshot/empty-guard specified.

roles.verdict: Conditional GO. The core model (visible = ENTITLED ∧ NOT killed ∧ ENABLED, preference only narrows) is correct and money-safe — preferences live in side tables that can never widen a billing entitlement, which is exactly right. But the migration has a real RLS bug (unqualified helper calls), the resolver as specified bypasses EVA's actual entitlement resolution path (team-pool + kill-switch), and the plan has NO backfill/grandfathering strategy — which is the concrete way current coaches break. Fix those three before building. The fintech surface itself (coach_addons / enabled_modules / webhook write-through) is correctly left untouched, which is the most important thing.

topConcerns.title: No backfill = silent feature loss for every existing coach AND alumno on day 1
topConcerns.detail: The resolver reads coach_feature_prefs; existing coaches have NO row. If 'no row' resolves to the new defaults (advanced sections OFF), then every coach already working nutrition deeply (recetas, lista de compras, hábitos, registro fuera de plan) loses those surfaces the instant this ships — and their alumnos see sections vanish mid-relationship. This is the canonical 'a column default does not back-populate existing rows' trap. The plan's F0-F2 never mentions a backfill migration. REQUIRED: a separate, batched, idempotent backfill that derives each coach's default from OBSERVED USAGE (has nutrition_meal_logs / non-empty plans with recetas/habitos rows → grandfather those sections ON; preset='intermedio' or 'profesional'), and treats truly-empty coaches as 'basico'. Grandfather existing = ON, new coaches = OFF. Without this you break exactly the coaches who use EVA most.

topConcerns.title: Migration RLS bug: pool helpers called unqualified (current_user_pool_coach_ids() not public.…)
topConcerns.detail: Lines 37 and 64 of 20260618200000_feature_prefs.sql call current_user_pool_coach_ids() / current_user_pool_client_ids() WITHOUT the public. schema prefix. Every other EVA migration qualifies them (see 20260609160000, 20260609170000 — always 'SELECT public.current_user_pool_client_ids()'). RLS policy bodies evaluate under the QUERYING role's search_path, not the function's SET search_path, so an unqualified call can fail to resolve at runtime for pool (team/Movida) coaches → policy errors or zero rows → team coaches' prefs silently broken while standalone works. Qualify both with public. and add a pool-context RLS test (tests/team/*) before merge, since Movida is the live multi-coach pool tenant.

topConcerns.title: Resolver signature drops EVA's real entitlement path (team-pool wins + operator kill-switch)
topConcerns.detail: The PLAN's resolveFeaturePrefs(coachId, clientId?, domain) reduces the Pro gate to a coach-level proEnabled. But real EVA entitlement for nutrition_exchanges resolves by RESOURCE CONTEXT: hasExchangesModuleForClientContext / findPlanModuleContext where the team pool's teams.enabled_modules WINS over coaches.enabled_modules, then applyOperatorKillSwitch (EVA_DISABLED_MODULES) sits above both. A bare coachId resolver will mis-gate Pro rows for pool alumnos (Movida) and ignore the kill-switch. The resolver MUST consume hasModule/hasExchangesModuleForClientContext with the same {coachId, clientTeamId, clientOrgId} context the live code uses — reuse it verbatim, do not reimplement. The plan text mentions 'NOT killed' but the resolver pseudocode does not thread it.

topConcerns.title: Three permissive policies on coach_feature_prefs (FOR ALL + 2 FOR SELECT) reignite the perf debt you just paid down
topConcerns.detail: coach_feature_prefs has owner_all (FOR ALL, which includes SELECT) + client_read (SELECT) + team_read (SELECT) = three permissive SELECT policies stacking on the same role/cmd. This is exactly the multiple_permissive_policies pattern the 2026-06 DB audit fought from 575→510. client_feature_prefs has the same shape (coach_all FOR ALL + client_read + team_all FOR ALL). Run get_advisors after apply; ideally consolidate (e.g. owner/coach into the SELECT path or split FOR ALL into INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE so SELECT has fewer overlapping permissive policies).

topConcerns.title: preset has no validation anywhere → resolver indexes undefined → section silently OFF
topConcerns.detail: preset is nullable text with no CHECK (deliberate for genericity, fine). But then sections[key].presets[coachPrefs.preset] will be undefined for a null/garbage preset, collapsing every optional section to OFF. The write-path server action MUST Zod-validate preset ∈ {basico,intermedio,profesional} per domain, and the resolver MUST coerce null/unknown → 'basico' as the safe floor. Make this explicit in F0/F1; it is one line of code but its absence is a real data-integrity hole.

topConcerns.title: Unconditional DROP TABLE of the two nutrition_prefs tables under forward-only replay
topConcerns.detail: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS coach_nutrition_prefs/client_nutrition_prefs with no emptiness guard. CLAUDE.md states merge_branch replays ALL history forward-only. The plan asserts they are empty 'aplicadas por error', but if any row ever lands before this ships, replay destroys it silently. Confirm 0 rows via list_tables/execute_sql immediately before apply, and add a defensive comment. Also regen database.types.ts after (some consumers may reference old names).

improvements.title: Add an explicit F0.5 backfill phase derived from usage signals
improvements.detail: Separate migration (additive, batched, throttled, re-runnable per merge_branch replay). Logic: for each coach, inspect existing nutrition usage (nutrition_plans with meals, nutrition_meal_logs, presence of recetas/habitos/shopping rows). Coaches with rich usage → preset 'profesional'/'intermedio' + grandfather the matching sections ON. Empty/new coaches → 'basico'. Alumno client_feature_prefs need NO backfill if client resolver falls back to coach prefs when client row absent (most-specific-wins: client override → coach pref → default). Make 'absent client row = inherit coach, do NOT force OFF' an explicit resolver rule and unit-test it.

improvements.title: Reuse hasExchangesModuleForClientContext / findPlanModuleContext in the resolver, don't reimplement the Pro gate
improvements.detail: entitlements.service.ts + nutrition-exchanges.service.ts already encode pool-wins + kill-switch + plan-context. The resolver's ENTITLED layer should call these with the real resource context so the new system inherits the money-correct behavior for free. This also keeps a single source of truth for 'does this coach/alumno have Nutrición Pro' — avoiding drift where the Funciones zone says Pro is available but the actual assertModule gate (server) rejects, or vice-versa (which would look like a billing bug to the coach).

improvements.title: Keep Funciones (preference) and Settings/Módulos (entitlement) visually and copy-distinct, reuse MODULE_CATALOG strings
improvements.detail: A coach will confuse 'I turned the Pro toggle on in Funciones' with 'I bought Pro'. The Funciones zone must (a) show Pro rows as locked-with-upsell when NOT entitled, reusing the exact MODULE_CATALOG copy/CTA from ModulesForm so pricing/pitch never drifts, and (b) carry a one-liner 'Esto NO cobra — habilita lo que ya tienes' so a toggle is never mistaken for a purchase. The entitlement catalog at /coach/settings/modules stays read-only and is the only money surface. This protects against support tickets that look like double-charge confusion.

improvements.title: Rename nutrition_set_updated_at → feature_prefs_set_updated_at (or reuse an existing generic set_updated_at)
improvements.detail: The trigger fn is nutrition-named on generic *_feature_prefs tables — contradicts the genericity thesis and will mislead the next dev into thinking these tables are nutrition-only. Grep for an existing generic set_updated_at before adding a new one.

improvements.title: Nav consolidation (Ejercicios → Programas) goes through NAV_MODULES + its tests, and keeps /coach/exercises alive
improvements.detail: Remove the 'exercises' entry from coach-nav.ts NAV_MODULES, add the colored 'Lista de ejercicios' button in WorkoutProgramsClient, but DO NOT delete the route (deep links + apps/mobile ejercicios tab + client /c/[slug]/exercises reference it). Update coach-nav.test.ts and any nav-title E2E spec (collectNavTitles) in the same change. If Ejercicios is meant to be a coach-toggleable base function (founder goal 2), add a featurePref hook to getVisibleNavItems parallel to the existing entitlement hook — the registry currently has no preference dimension.

improvements.title: Adopt the native '4 primary + Más' pattern for the web responsive bottom bar
improvements.detail: The single worst clutter spot is web mobile (8-10 flat horizontal-scroll tiles). apps/mobile already solved this (PRIMARY_TABS=4 + Más overflow in CoachMobileChrome.tsx). Mirror it: Inicio · Alumnos · Planes · Nutri · Más, with Marca/Plan/Soporte/Opciones folded behind Más and Ejercicios folded into Planes. This is independent of the prefs backend and delivers the founder's 'consolidate' goal directly.

roles.biggestRisk: Shipping the resolver without a usage-derived backfill: the moment the AND-ENABLED layer goes live, every existing coach with no prefs row has their advanced nutrition sections (and their alumnos' visible sections) collapse to the new OFF defaults — silently breaking exactly the power coaches who rely on EVA most, with no error and no audit trail. It mirrors EVA's own documented data-loss class (additive-but-behavior-changing migration) except the loss is UX/visibility rather than rows. The fix is non-negotiable: grandfather existing coaches ON from observed usage, default only NEW coaches OFF.
missing: No backfill / grandfathering migration for existing coaches and alumnos (the core 'don't break current coaches' gap)
Resolver does not thread team-pool entitlement (teams.enabled_modules wins) nor the EVA_DISABLED_MODULES operator kill-switch — both live in entitlements.service.ts and must be consumed, not bypassed
No 'absent client row = inherit coach prefs' rule specified → risk that per-alumno layer defaults everything OFF for alumnos whose coach never set an override
No Zod validation of preset/domain on the write path, and no resolver coercion of null/unknown preset → safe floor
No post-apply get_advisors run mentioned despite three stacked permissive policies per table (perf-regression risk against the 575→510 work already done)
No RLS isolation test for the pool/team (Movida) context — the unqualified-helper bug would only surface for pool coaches, exactly the live multi-coach tenant

No dependent-widget gating rule: turning Nutrición off must also hide dependent dashboard surfaces (MacroBar, ComplianceRing, NutritionDailySummary already consume nutrition) or the alumno sees orphaned/empty widgets

No explicit statement that a Funciones toggle writes ONLY to *_feature_prefs.sections jsonb and is FORBIDDEN from deleting/nulling any nutrition_* row (CASCADE-safety must be a PR-review gate, not just prose)
No regen of database.types.ts called out after the DROP + new tables
Unqualified pool-helper calls in the migration (public. prefix missing) — a concrete bug, not just a gap
